0000_migration-helpers.sql:
-- Idempotency helper procedures for znapi migrations.
-- Re-runnable: each DROPs then CREATEs (so 0000_ is exempt from checksum tracking).
-- VERIFIED against MySQL 9.4: all procedures load cleanly; SIGNAL-based asserts raise
-- ERROR 1644 on failure and are clean no-ops on success.
--
-- ADD NEW HELPERS HERE as migrations need them — growing this file is safe because the
-- runner re-applies 0000_ on every run (Task 10 discovery exemption).

-- ── Structural guards ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

DROP PROCEDURE IF EXISTS zn_add_index_if_missing
DROP PROCEDURE IF EXISTS zn_replace_check
DROP PROCEDURE IF EXISTS zn_add_column_if_missing
DROP PROCEDURE IF EXISTS zn_drop_column_if_exists
DROP PROCEDURE IF EXISTS zn_add_unique_if_missing

-- ── Postcondition assertions ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
-- These RAISE (SIGNAL SQLSTATE '45000' → ERROR 1644) when the asserted end-state is
-- absent. JDBC execute() surfaces that as a SQLException, so the run ABORTS and the
-- row stays success=0. VERIFIED against MySQL 9.4: a passing assert is a clean no-op;
-- a failing one stops the script. MESSAGE_TEXT MUST be a variable (a CONCAT expression
-- there is a syntax error). Every migration ends with one or more CALL zn_assert_*.

DROP PROCEDURE IF EXISTS zn_assert_column
DROP PROCEDURE IF EXISTS zn_assert_table
DROP PROCEDURE IF EXISTS zn_assert_index
DROP PROCEDURE IF EXISTS zn_assert_check_contains
DROP PROCEDURE IF EXISTS zn_drop_fk_if_exists
DROP PROCEDURE IF EXISTS zn_assert_fk_absent
2026-06-30_001_residual-historial-state-:
-- Add per-action state metadata to the residual-container audit log so supervisors can
-- see the "where / into what" of each transition directly in the container dialog, not
-- just the bare status change. Previously a UBICAR row didn't record the location it was
-- placed at, and a VACIAR row didn't record the compactor it was emptied into.
--
--   location_id     INT NULL          — location involved in the action:
--                                         UBICAR  → where it was placed
--                                         RETIRAR → where it was removed from (cleared)
--                                         VACIAR  → where it had been (cleared on empty)
--   compactor_id    INT NULL          — compactor used by a VACIAR action
--   content_percent DECIMAL(10,2) NULL — fill % recorded at RETIRAR (mirrors the container's
--                                         current_content_percent column type)
--
-- The acting USER is already captured by ZnNonUpdatableEntity's `created_by` column
-- (FK to empresa_usuario), and the acting TEAM by the existing `team_id` — so no new
-- actor columns are needed. All three new columns are nullable: most actions populate
-- none of them, and historical rows have none.
--
-- Idempotent via zn_add_column_if_missing; safe to re-run.

CALL zn_add_column_if_missing('mwm_contenedor_residual_historial', 'location_id',     'INT NULL AFTER team_id')
2026-06-30_002_mwm-retirada-numero-di.sq:
-- Separate the DI's own document number from the `dcs` column on `mwm_retirada`.
--
-- The La Paz importer was writing TWO different things into `dcs`:
--   * real DCS codes (eSIR, "DCS" + ~24 digits) — a globally-unique official id, and
--   * the DI's own short number for removals with NO DCS:
--       - non-hazardous DIs carrying the gestor's own number (e.g. "21756"), and
--       - the Madrid regional "Nº de documento" (e.g. "0097928").
-- Those short numbers are NOT unique across gestores, so two unrelated removals can
-- reuse the same number. With `dcs` under a UNIQUE (company_id, dcs) constraint, the
-- second such removal was REJECTED by the DB (surfaced as an ItemFailure). And the UI
-- showed "Nº DCS" over values that aren't DCS.
--
-- Fix: a dedicated `numero_di` column (NOT unique) for the DI's own number, leaving
-- `dcs` (and its UNIQUE) reserved for real DCS codes only. The importer now writes
-- each value to the correct column based on RetiradaIdKind.
--
--   numero_di  VARCHAR(50) NULL  — the DI's own short document number; NULL for
--                                  removals identified by a real DCS.
--
-- Idempotent: the ADD COLUMN / ADD INDEX use the zn_*_if_missing helpers. The data
-- UPDATE is guarded so it only moves values that are unambiguously a DI number, never
-- a real DCS. Re-running it is harmless (already-moved rows have dcs = NULL).
--
-- ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
-- ⚠️  BEFORE APPLYING IN PRODUCTION — run this audit SELECT and eyeball the result.
--     It classifies every dcs value; the UPDATE below only moves the NUMERO_DI bucket.
--     If the AMBIGUO bucket is non-empty, STOP and inspect those rows by hand before
--     migrating (a real DCS that is purely numeric and short would be misclassified).
--     Also take a backup of mwm_retirada (at least removal_id, company_id, dcs) — the
--     `dcs = NULL` part of the UPDATE is NOT reversible without it.
--
--   SELECT
--     CASE
--       WHEN dcs LIKE 'DCS%' OR CHAR_LENGTH(dcs) >= 20 THEN 'DCS_REAL'
--       WHEN dcs REGEXP '^[0-9]+$' AND CHAR_LENGTH(dcs) <= 14 THEN 'NUMERO_DI'
--       ELSE 'AMBIGUO'
--     END AS clasif,
--     COUNT(*) AS n, MIN(CHAR_LENGTH(dcs)) AS min_len, MAX(CHAR_LENGTH(dcs)) AS max_len
--   FROM mwm_retirada
--   WHERE dcs IS NOT NULL
--   GROUP BY clasif;
-- ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

-- 1. New column + non-unique index (short DI numbers legitimately repeat).
CALL zn_add_column_if_missing('mwm_retirada', 'numero_di', 'VARCHAR(50) NULL DEFAULT NULL AFTER dcs')

-- 2. Migrate existing data: move ONLY the values that are unambiguously a DI number
--    (purely numeric, <= 14 chars, not DCS-prefixed) from `dcs` to `numero_di`, and
--    clear `dcs` for those rows. Real DCS codes (DCS-prefixed or >= 20 digits) stay
--    put. This relies on the confirmed invariant: no real DCS is purely numeric and
--    short. The audit SELECT above is the safeguard.
UPDATE mwm_retirada
   SET numero_di = dcs,
       dcs = NULL
 WHERE dcs IS NOT NULL
   AND dcs NOT LIKE 'DCS%'
   AND dcs REGEXP '^[0-9]+$'
   AND CHAR_LENGTH(dcs) <= 14
2026-06-30_003_import-plan-job-status.sq:
-- Make the La Paz import plan staging JOB-AWARE so analysis can run asynchronously.
--
-- The analyze endpoint was synchronous; with Mistral OCR a large import exceeds the
-- Cloudflare request cap (504). We move analysis to a background worker that writes the
-- plan + a status here, and the frontend polls. The plan was already persisted to
-- `_import_plan`; this adds the durable JOB STATE so polling has something to read.
--
--   status         ENUM-like VARCHAR  — PENDING (job queued, no plan yet)
--                                       PROCESSING (worker running)
--                                       READY (plan_json populated, usable)
--                                       FAILED (see error_message)
--   error_message  TEXT NULL          — failure reason when status = FAILED
--
-- `plan_json` becomes NULLABLE: a PENDING/PROCESSING row exists before the plan does.
-- Existing rows are all completed plans, so they're backfilled to status = 'READY'.
--
-- Idempotent via the zn_*_if_missing helpers; safe to re-run.

-- 1. New columns. status defaults to READY so any plan written by the OLD synchronous
--    path (which doesn't set status) is immediately usable.
CALL zn_add_column_if_missing('_import_plan', 'status', "VARCHAR(16) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'READY' AFTER item_count")

-- 2. Allow a job row to exist before its plan is computed.
ALTER TABLE `_import_plan` MODIFY COLUMN `plan_json` longtext COLLATE utf8mb4_unicode_ci NULL

-- 3. Index for the worker/sweep to find jobs by status quickly.
CALL zn_add_index_if_missing('_import_plan', 'idx_import_plan_status', 'status,expires_at')

-- 4. Backfill: every pre-existing row is a finished plan.
UPDATE `_import_plan` SET status = 'READY' WHERE status IS NULL OR status = ''
archive/2026-02-06_001_query-definitions:
-- Database-Driven Query Definitions Schema
-- This migration creates tables for storing customizable query definitions
-- that can be managed via an admin UI instead of hardcoded TypeScript files.

-- Main query definition
CREATE TABLE query_definition (
    query_id INT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
    company_id INT NOT NULL,
    module_name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,          -- e.g., 'albaranes.albaranes'
    query_key VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,            -- e.g., 'chronological-archive'
    name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    base_query_template VARCHAR(500),           -- e.g., 'a.ejercicio = {{currentYear}}'
    base_order VARCHAR(200),
    filter_set_key VARCHAR(100),                -- Reference to code filter set
    order_set_key VARCHAR(100),                 -- Reference to code order set
    load_ns JSON,                               -- Namespaces to load for i18n
    is_active BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE,
    created_by VARCHAR(100),
    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    updated_by VARCHAR(100),
    updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    UNIQUE KEY uk_module_query (company_id, module_name, query_key),
    INDEX idx_module_name (company_id, module_name),
    INDEX idx_is_active (is_active)
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci

-- Query tabs (each ModuleQuery has multiple Query tabs/sheets)
CREATE TABLE query_tab (
    tab_id INT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
    query_id INT NOT NULL,
    tab_key VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,              -- Unique key within the query
    name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,                 -- Display name (i18n key)
    ql TEXT NOT NULL,                           -- HQL query
    views JSON NOT NULL,                        -- e.g., '["Albaran"]' - entity views for export
    where_clause TEXT,                          -- Additional WHERE clause
    sort_order INT DEFAULT 0,
    is_active BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE,
    FOREIGN KEY (query_id) REFERENCES query_definition(query_id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    UNIQUE KEY uk_query_tab (query_id, tab_key),
    INDEX idx_sort_order (query_id, sort_order)
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci

-- Column definitions for each tab
CREATE TABLE query_column (
    column_id INT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
    tab_id INT NOT NULL,
    property VARCHAR(200) NOT NULL,             -- Column property path (e.g., 'albaran_date')
    name VARCHAR(100),                          -- i18n key for column header
    header VARCHAR(200),                        -- Direct header text (fallback if no i18n)
    template_key VARCHAR(100),                  -- Reference to code template (e.g., 'dateShort', 'currency')
    date_type ENUM('date', 'timestamp'),        -- Date type for Excel export
    sort_column VARCHAR(200),                   -- Column to sort by (if different from property)
    width VARCHAR(50),                          -- CSS width
    xl_style_key VARCHAR(100),                  -- Reference to XL style template
    css_class VARCHAR(100),                     -- CSS class for styling
    css_class_expr VARCHAR(500),                -- Expression for dynamic CSS class (e.g., conditional styling)
    is_visible BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE,
    sort_order INT DEFAULT 0,
    extra_config JSON,                          -- Additional configuration (getters, custom plugins, etc.)
    FOREIGN KEY (tab_id) REFERENCES query_tab(tab_id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    INDEX idx_sort_order (tab_id, sort_order)
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci

-- Add comments for documentation
ALTER TABLE query_definition COMMENT = 'Stores configurable query definitions for modules'
ALTER TABLE query_tab COMMENT = 'Query tabs/sheets within a query definition'
ALTER TABLE query_column COMMENT = 'Column definitions for query tabs'
archive/2026-02-25_001_fulltext-ngram-in:
-- =============================================================================
-- MySQL FULLTEXT n-gram Index Migration
-- =============================================================================
--
-- Prerequisites:
--   1. ngram_token_size=2 must be set in MySQL my.cnf (this is the default).
--      Verify with: SHOW VARIABLES LIKE 'ngram_token_size';
--   2. Stopwords MUST be disabled before creating n-gram indexes.
--      The InnoDB default stopword list contains short words ('an', 'in', 'or', etc.)
--      that collide with common bigrams, causing searches to silently return 0 results.
--
-- Per-column indexes (not composite) because MATCH() can only reference columns
-- from one FULLTEXT index, and the frontend dynamically selects different column
-- combinations per search.
--
-- Non-blocking on MySQL 8+ InnoDB (online DDL).
-- InnoDB Cluster compatible — DDL propagates to all nodes.
-- Without these indexes, MATCH() will throw an error (not silently fall back).
-- =============================================================================

-- CRITICAL: Disable stopwords before creating n-gram FULLTEXT indexes.
-- Without this, bigrams like 'an' (from 'Kanali') are silently filtered,
-- causing MATCH AGAINST to return 0 results for affected terms.
SET SESSION innodb_ft_enable_stopword = OFF

-- Albaran direct fields
ALTER TABLE albaran ADD FULLTEXT INDEX ft_albaran_cliente (albaran_cliente) WITH PARSER ngram
ALTER TABLE albaran ADD FULLTEXT INDEX ft_matricula (matricula) WITH PARSER ngram
ALTER TABLE albaran ADD FULLTEXT INDEX ft_remolque (remolque) WITH PARSER ngram
ALTER TABLE albaran ADD FULLTEXT INDEX ft_esn (ejercicio_serie_numero) WITH PARSER ngram

-- Entidad (referenced by Albaran/Prefactura via JOINs)
ALTER TABLE entidad ADD FULLTEXT INDEX ft_nombre (nombre) WITH PARSER ngram
ALTER TABLE entidad ADD FULLTEXT INDEX ft_cif (cif) WITH PARSER ngram

-- EntidadCentro
ALTER TABLE entidad_centro ADD FULLTEXT INDEX ft_nombre (nombre) WITH PARSER ngram

-- AlbaranLinea (flattened collection search)
ALTER TABLE albaran_linea ADD FULLTEXT INDEX ft_descripcion (descripcion) WITH PARSER ngram

-- Prefactura
ALTER TABLE prefactura ADD FULLTEXT INDEX ft_esn (ejercicio_serie_numero) WITH PARSER ngram
ALTER TABLE prefactura ADD FULLTEXT INDEX ft_descripcion (descripcion) WITH PARSER ngram

-- PrefacturaLinea
ALTER TABLE prefactura_linea ADD FULLTEXT INDEX ft_descripcion (descripcion) WITH PARSER ngram
archive/2026-02-25_002_permission-profil:
-- Permission Profiles (Perfiles de Permiso) migration
-- Adds role-based permission profiles that can be assigned to multiple users.
-- Run against each company database.

-- 1. Role definition table
CREATE TABLE perfil_permiso (
    company_id              INT NOT NULL,
    profile_permission_id   INT NOT NULL,
    nombre                  VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    descripcion             VARCHAR(500) NULL,
    activo                  BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT TRUE,
    created_by              VARCHAR(45) NULL,
    created_at              DATETIME NULL,
    updated_by              VARCHAR(45) NULL,
    updated_at              DATETIME NULL,
    PRIMARY KEY (company_id, profile_permission_id),
    UNIQUE KEY uk_perfil_nombre (company_id, nombre),
    FOREIGN KEY (company_id) REFERENCES empresa(company_id) ON DELETE CASCADE
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4

-- 2. Role → module permissions (mirrors empresa_usuario_acl structure)
CREATE TABLE perfil_permiso_acl (
    company_id              INT NOT NULL,
    profile_permission_id   INT NOT NULL,
    `module`                VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
    permissions             JSON NULL,
    settings                JSON NULL,
    PRIMARY KEY (company_id, profile_permission_id, `module`),
    FOREIGN KEY (company_id, profile_permission_id)
        REFERENCES perfil_permiso(company_id, profile_permission_id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    FOREIGN KEY (`module`)
        REFERENCES _module(`module`) ON UPDATE CASCADE
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4

-- 3. User ↔ Role junction table
CREATE TABLE empresa_usuario_perfil (
    company_id              INT NOT NULL,
    user_name               VARCHAR(45) NOT NULL,
    profile_permission_id   INT NOT NULL,
    PRIMARY KEY (company_id, user_name, profile_permission_id),
    FOREIGN KEY (company_id, user_name)
        REFERENCES empresa_usuario(company_id, user_name) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    FOREIGN KEY (company_id, profile_permission_id)
        REFERENCES perfil_permiso(company_id, profile_permission_id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    INDEX idx_eup_profile (company_id, profile_permission_id)
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4

-- 4. Add deny_permissions column to existing user ACL table
ALTER TABLE empresa_usuario_acl
    ADD COLUMN deny_permissions JSON NULL AFTER permissions

-- Migration: CuentaAjuste (Accrual Adjustment Entity)
-- Temporal redistribution of amounts within the same account

CREATE TABLE cuenta_ajuste (
    company_id INT NOT NULL,
    adjustment_id INT NOT NULL,
    account_id INT NOT NULL,
    fecha DATE NOT NULL,
    fecha_destino DATE NOT NULL,
    cantidad DECIMAL(15,2) NOT NULL,
    comentarios VARCHAR(500),
    auto_reverse_fecha DATE NULL,
    master_id INT NULL,
    category_id INT NULL,
    created_by VARCHAR(45),
    created_at DATETIME,
    updated_by VARCHAR(45),
    updated_at DATETIME,
    PRIMARY KEY (company_id, adjustment_id)
)

ALTER TABLE cuenta_linea
    ADD COLUMN adjustment_id INT NULL AFTER transfer_id,
    ADD COLUMN auto_reverse BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT FALSE AFTER adjustment_id
CREATE INDEX idx_cl_adjustment ON cuenta_linea(company_id, adjustment_id)
-- Payroll System Redesign Migration
-- Adds surrogate key + status workflow to nomina_lote, FK from empleado_nomina to nomina_lote
-- Run this BEFORE deploying the new backend code

-- ============================================================
-- Phase 1: Add surrogate key + new columns to nomina_lote
-- ============================================================

-- Add surrogate auto-increment key
ALTER TABLE `zincdb`.`nomina_lote` ADD COLUMN `payroll_batch_id` int NULL AFTER `company_id`
SET @current_company := NULL
SET @row_number := 0

UPDATE nomina_lote nl
    JOIN (
    SELECT
    company_id,
    fecha,
    @row_number := IF(@current_company = company_id, @row_number + 1, 1) AS new_batch_id,
    @current_company := company_id
    FROM nomina_lote
    ORDER BY company_id, fecha
    ) seq
ON nl.company_id = seq.company_id
    AND nl.fecha = seq.fecha
    SET nl.payroll_batch_id = seq.new_batch_id

ALTER TABLE `zincdb`.`nomina_lote`
    DROP PRIMARY KEY,
    ADD PRIMARY KEY (`company_id`, `payroll_batch_id`) USING BTREE

-- Add status workflow
ALTER TABLE nomina_lote ADD COLUMN status VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'DRAFT'

-- Add batch-level fields (exist in frontend but not backend)
ALTER TABLE nomina_lote ADD COLUMN payroll_type_id INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 1
ALTER TABLE nomina_lote ADD COLUMN master_id INT DEFAULT NULL
ALTER TABLE nomina_lote ADD COLUMN category_id INT DEFAULT NULL
ALTER TABLE nomina_lote ADD COLUMN mean_id INT DEFAULT NULL
ALTER TABLE nomina_lote ADD COLUMN contabilizar TINYINT(1) NOT NULL DEFAULT 1
ALTER TABLE nomina_lote ADD COLUMN vencimiento DATE DEFAULT NULL
ALTER TABLE nomina_lote ADD COLUMN descripcion VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL DEFAULT ''
ALTER TABLE nomina_lote ADD COLUMN ejercicio INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0

-- Keep fecha as a regular indexed column
ALTER TABLE nomina_lote ADD INDEX idx_nomina_lote_fecha (company_id, fecha)

ALTER TABLE `zincdb`.`nomina_lote`
    ADD CONSTRAINT `fk_nomina_lote_category` FOREIGN KEY (`company_id`, `master_id`, `category_id`) REFERENCES `zincdb`.`cuenta_categoria` (`company_id`, `master_id`, `category_id`) ON DELETE RESTRICT ON UPDATE CASCADE,
    ADD CONSTRAINT `fk_nomina_lote_nomina_lote` FOREIGN KEY (`payroll_type_id`) REFERENCES `zincdb`.`_nomina_tipo` (`payroll_type_id`) ON DELETE RESTRICT ON UPDATE CASCADE,
    ADD CONSTRAINT `fk_nomina_lote_medio_pago` FOREIGN KEY (`company_id`, `mean_id`) REFERENCES `zincdb`.`medio_pago` (`company_id`, `mean_id`) ON DELETE RESTRICT ON UPDATE CASCADE

-- ============================================================
-- Phase 2: Add FK column to empleado_nomina
-- ============================================================

ALTER TABLE empleado_nomina ADD COLUMN payroll_batch_id INT DEFAULT NULL
ALTER TABLE empleado_nomina ADD INDEX idx_en_batch (company_id, payroll_batch_id)

-- ============================================================
-- Phase 3: Data migration (populate FK from matching fecha)
-- ============================================================

UPDATE empleado_nomina en
JOIN nomina_lote nl ON en.company_id = nl.company_id AND en.fecha = nl.fecha
SET en.payroll_batch_id = nl.payroll_batch_id

-- Set status for existing batches
UPDATE nomina_lote SET status = 'PAID' WHERE pago IS NOT NULL
UPDATE nomina_lote SET status = 'APPROVED' WHERE pago IS NULL AND status = 'DRAFT'

-- Set ejercicio from fecha year
UPDATE nomina_lote SET ejercicio = YEAR(fecha) WHERE ejercicio = 0

-- ============================================================
-- Phase 4: Create batches for orphaned payrolls
-- ============================================================

-- Insert new batches for orphaned payrolls (those with no matching fecha in nomina_lote)
INSERT INTO nomina_lote (company_id, fecha, cantidad, nominas, pendiente, seguridad_social, irpf, status, ejercicio, comentarios)
SELECT
    en.company_id,
    en.fecha,
    SUM(en.importe),
    COUNT(*),
    SUM(en.pendiente),
    SUM(en.seguridad_social),
    SUM(en.irpf),
    CASE WHEN MAX(CASE WHEN en.pago IS NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) = 0 THEN 'PAID' ELSE 'APPROVED' END,
    YEAR(en.fecha),
    ''
FROM empleado_nomina en
WHERE en.payroll_batch_id IS NULL
GROUP BY en.company_id, en.fecha

-- Link the newly created batches
UPDATE empleado_nomina en
JOIN nomina_lote nl ON en.company_id = nl.company_id AND en.fecha = nl.fecha
SET en.payroll_batch_id = nl.payroll_batch_id
WHERE en.payroll_batch_id IS NULL

ALTER TABLE `zincdb`.`empleado_nomina`
    ADD CONSTRAINT `fk_emp_nomina_nomina_lote` FOREIGN KEY (`company_id`, `payroll_batch_id`) REFERENCES `zincdb`.`nomina_lote` (`company_id`, `payroll_batch_id`) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE

-- ============================================================
-- Phase 5: Add empleados (distinct employee count) aggregate
-- ============================================================
ALTER TABLE nomina_lote ADD COLUMN empleados INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0

UPDATE nomina_lote nl SET empleados = (
    SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT en.employee_id) FROM empleado_nomina en
    WHERE en.company_id = nl.company_id AND en.payroll_batch_id = nl.payroll_batch_id
)
archive/2026-03-01_002_residual-containe:
-- ========================================
-- 1. Fix residual_container_id type: VARCHAR(50) → INT across all tables
-- ========================================

-- Drop FKs referencing the parent column
ALTER TABLE mwm_compactador_evento DROP FOREIGN KEY fk_mwm_compact_evt_residual
ALTER TABLE mwm_contenedor_residual_evento_llenado DROP FOREIGN KEY fk_mwm_cont_res_evt_cont
ALTER TABLE mwm_contenedor_residual_historial DROP FOREIGN KEY fk_mwm_cont_res_hist_cont

-- Alter child tables first
ALTER TABLE mwm_compactador_evento MODIFY COLUMN residual_container_id INT NOT NULL
ALTER TABLE mwm_contenedor_residual_evento_llenado MODIFY COLUMN residual_container_id INT NOT NULL

-- Alter parent table
ALTER TABLE mwm_contenedor_residual MODIFY COLUMN residual_container_id INT NOT NULL

-- Re-add FKs
ALTER TABLE mwm_compactador_evento
    ADD CONSTRAINT fk_mwm_compact_evt_residual
    FOREIGN KEY (company_id, client_id, center_id, residual_container_id)
    REFERENCES mwm_contenedor_residual (company_id, client_id, center_id, residual_container_id)

ALTER TABLE mwm_contenedor_residual_evento_llenado
    ADD CONSTRAINT fk_mwm_cont_res_evt_cont
    FOREIGN KEY (company_id, client_id, center_id, residual_container_id)
    REFERENCES mwm_contenedor_residual (company_id, client_id, center_id, residual_container_id)

-- ========================================
-- 2. Drop and recreate historial (audit log) table with new schema
-- ========================================
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS mwm_contenedor_residual_historial

CREATE TABLE mwm_contenedor_residual_historial (
    company_id INT NOT NULL,
    client_id INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    center_id INT NOT NULL,
    residual_container_id INT NOT NULL,
    residual_container_history_id INT NOT NULL,
    action VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
    previous_status_id INT NULL,
    new_status_id INT NOT NULL,
    team_id INT NULL,
    notes TEXT NULL,
    created_by VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    created_at DATETIME(6) NOT NULL,
    PRIMARY KEY (company_id, client_id, center_id, residual_container_id, residual_container_history_id),
    CONSTRAINT fk_historial_container FOREIGN KEY (company_id, client_id, center_id, residual_container_id)
        REFERENCES mwm_contenedor_residual (company_id, client_id, center_id, residual_container_id),
    CONSTRAINT fk_historial_team FOREIGN KEY (company_id, team_id)
        REFERENCES mwm_equipo (company_id, team_id),
    INDEX idx_historial_container (company_id, client_id, center_id, residual_container_id),
    INDEX idx_historial_action (action)
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci
archive/2026-03-01_003_printable-version:
-- Printable Style & Series Versioning
-- Stores snapshots of previous state before each update

CREATE TABLE _printable_style_version (
    version_id INT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
    style      VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    definition LONGTEXT,
    preview    LONGBLOB,

archive/2026-03-02_001_fix-has-albaran-f:
-- Fix hasAlbaran flag on prefactura and factura tables.
-- The flag was not being updated correctly on link/unlink operations,
-- so existing data may be inconsistent.
--
-- This script reconciles has_albaran against actual albaran FK references.

-- 1. Prefactura: has_albaran = true if any albaran references it
UPDATE prefactura p
SET p.has_albaran = EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 FROM albaran a
    WHERE a.company_id = p.company_id
      AND a.preinvoice_id = p.preinvoice_id
)

-- 2. Factura: has_albaran = true if any albaran references it
UPDATE factura f
SET f.has_albaran = EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 FROM albaran a
    WHERE a.company_id = f.company_id
      AND a.invoice_id = f.invoice_id
)

-- Migration: Obra (Project Management) + Albaran Service Fields
-- Date: 2026-03-08
-- Description: Adds project management (Obra) tables and service/DI fields to Albaran

-- =============================================================================
-- 1. Obra tables
-- =============================================================================

CREATE TABLE obra (
    company_id INT NOT NULL,
    obra_id INT NOT NULL,
    nombre VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    descripcion TEXT,
    client_id INT NULL,
    center_id INT NULL,
    fecha_inicio DATE NULL,
    fecha_fin DATE NULL,
    estado VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'abierta',
    presupuesto DECIMAL(15,2) NULL,
    observaciones TEXT,
    created_at TIMESTAMP NULL,
    created_by VARCHAR(45) NULL,
    updated_at TIMESTAMP NULL,
    updated_by VARCHAR(45) NULL,
    PRIMARY KEY (company_id, obra_id),
    CONSTRAINT fk_obra_company FOREIGN KEY (company_id) REFERENCES empresa(company_id) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE,
    CONSTRAINT fk_obra_client FOREIGN KEY (company_id, client_id) REFERENCES entidad(company_id, client_id) ON DELETE RESTRICT ON UPDATE CASCADE,
    CONSTRAINT fk_obra_center FOREIGN KEY (company_id, client_id, center_id) REFERENCES entidad_centro(company_id, client_id, center_id) ON DELETE RESTRICT ON UPDATE CASCADE,
    CONSTRAINT fk_obra_created_by FOREIGN KEY (company_id, created_by) REFERENCES `empresa_usuario` (`company_id`, `user_name`) ON DELETE RESTRICT ON UPDATE CASCADE,
    CONSTRAINT fk_obra_updated_by FOREIGN KEY (company_id, updated_by) REFERENCES `empresa_usuario` (`company_id`, `user_name`) ON DELETE RESTRICT ON UPDATE CASCADE
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci

CREATE TABLE obra_material (
    company_id INT NOT NULL,
    obra_id INT NOT NULL,
    material_id INT NOT NULL,
    product_id INT NULL,
    cantidad DECIMAL(15,4) NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    precio DECIMAL(15,4) NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    descripcion VARCHAR(500) NULL,
    created_at TIMESTAMP NULL,
    created_by VARCHAR(45) NULL,
    updated_at TIMESTAMP NULL,
    updated_by VARCHAR(45) NULL,
    PRIMARY KEY (company_id, obra_id, material_id),
    CONSTRAINT fk_obra_material_obra FOREIGN KEY (company_id, obra_id) REFERENCES obra(company_id, obra_id) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE,
    CONSTRAINT fk_obra_material_product FOREIGN KEY (company_id, product_id) REFERENCES producto(company_id, product_id) ON DELETE RESTRICT ON UPDATE CASCADE,
    CONSTRAINT fk_obra_material_created_by FOREIGN KEY (company_id, created_by) REFERENCES `empresa_usuario` (`company_id`, `user_name`) ON DELETE RESTRICT ON UPDATE CASCADE,
    CONSTRAINT fk_obra_material_updated_by FOREIGN KEY (company_id, updated_by) REFERENCES `empresa_usuario` (`company_id`, `user_name`) ON DELETE RESTRICT ON UPDATE CASCADE
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci

CREATE TABLE obra_comentario (
    company_id INT NOT NULL,
    obra_id INT NOT NULL,
    comment_id INT NOT NULL,
    texto TEXT NOT NULL,
    created_at TIMESTAMP NULL,
    created_by VARCHAR(45) NULL,
    updated_at TIMESTAMP NULL,
    updated_by VARCHAR(45) NULL,
    PRIMARY KEY (company_id, obra_id, comment_id),
    CONSTRAINT fk_obra_comentario_obra FOREIGN KEY (company_id, obra_id) REFERENCES obra(company_id, obra_id) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE,
    CONSTRAINT fk_obra_comentario_created_by FOREIGN KEY (company_id, created_by) REFERENCES `empresa_usuario` (`company_id`, `user_name`) ON DELETE RESTRICT ON UPDATE CASCADE,
    CONSTRAINT fk_obra_comentario_updated_by FOREIGN KEY (company_id, updated_by) REFERENCES `empresa_usuario` (`company_id`, `user_name`) ON DELETE RESTRICT ON UPDATE CASCADE
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci

archive/2026-03-11_001_leave-system.sql:
-- ============================================================================
-- Leave Management System Migration
-- Migrates from hardcoded vacation/hours to generalized leave types
-- ============================================================================
-- IMPORTANT: Run in a maintenance window. Back up database first.
-- This migration must be run per-database (multi-tenant, one DB per company).
-- ============================================================================

-- ── 1. Create empleado_tipo_ausencia ─────────────────────────────────────────

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS empleado_tipo_ausencia (
    `company_id`            INT NOT NULL,
    `leave_type_id`         INT NOT NULL,
    `nombre`                VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    `unidad`                ENUM('DIA', 'HORA') NOT NULL DEFAULT 'DIA',
    `color`                 VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL DEFAULT '#4CAF50',
    `icono`                 VARCHAR(100),
    `medio_dia_permitido`   TINYINT(1) NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    `requiere_aprobacion`   TINYINT(1) NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
    `usa_horario`           TINYINT(1) NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
    `activo`                TINYINT(1) NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
    `orden`                 INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    `updated_at`            TIMESTAMP NULL DEFAULT NULL,
    `updated_by`            VARCHAR(45) CHARACTER SET utf8mb4 COLLATE utf8mb4_unicode_ci DEFAULT NULL,
    `created_at`            TIMESTAMP NULL DEFAULT NULL,
    `created_by`            VARCHAR(45) CHARACTER SET utf8mb4 COLLATE utf8mb4_unicode_ci DEFAULT NULL,
    PRIMARY KEY (company_id, leave_type_id),
    CONSTRAINT `fk_emp_tipo_aus_empresa`    FOREIGN KEY (`company_id`) REFERENCES `empresa` (`company_id`) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE,
    CONSTRAINT `fk_emp_tipo_aus_updated_by` FOREIGN KEY (`company_id`, `updated_by`) REFERENCES `empresa_usuario` (`company_id`, `user_name`) ON DELETE RESTRICT ON UPDATE CASCADE,
    CONSTRAINT `fk_emp_tipo_aus_created_by` FOREIGN KEY (`company_id`, `created_by`) REFERENCES `empresa_usuario` (`company_id`, `user_name`) ON DELETE RESTRICT ON UPDATE CASCADE,
    KEY idx_tipo_aus_auto (leave_type_id)
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci ROW_FORMAT=DYNAMIC

-- ── 2. Seed default leave types per company ──────────────────────────────────
-- Type 1 = Vacaciones (DIA), Type 2 = Horas personales (HORA)

INSERT IGNORE INTO empleado_tipo_ausencia (company_id, leave_type_id, nombre, unidad, color, medio_dia_permitido, requiere_aprobacion, usa_horario, activo, orden)
SELECT DISTINCT company_id, 1, 'Vacaciones', 'DIA', '#4CAF50', 1, 1, 1, 1, 0
FROM empleado_vacaciones

INSERT IGNORE INTO empleado_tipo_ausencia (company_id, leave_type_id, nombre, unidad, color, medio_dia_permitido, requiere_aprobacion, usa_horario, activo, orden)
SELECT DISTINCT company_id, 2, 'Horas personales', 'HORA', '#2196F3', 0, 1, 0, 1, 1
FROM empleado_vacaciones

-- ── 3. Create empleado_ausencia_balance ──────────────────────────────────────

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS empleado_ausencia_balance (
    company_id      INT NOT NULL,
    employee_id     INT NOT NULL,
    `year`          INT NOT NULL,
    leave_type_id   INT NOT NULL,
    asignadas       DECIMAL(10,2) NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    ajustadas       DECIMAL(10,2) NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    escogidas       DECIMAL(10,2) NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    disponibles     DECIMAL(10,2) NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    schedule_id     INT,
    `updated_at`    TIMESTAMP NULL DEFAULT NULL,
    `updated_by`    VARCHAR(45) CHARACTER SET utf8mb4 COLLATE utf8mb4_unicode_ci DEFAULT NULL,
    `created_at`    TIMESTAMP NULL DEFAULT NULL,
    `created_by`    VARCHAR(45) CHARACTER SET utf8mb4 COLLATE utf8mb4_unicode_ci DEFAULT NULL,
    PRIMARY KEY (company_id, employee_id, `year`, leave_type_id),
    KEY idx_aus_bal_schedule (company_id, schedule_id),
    KEY idx_aus_bal_type (company_id, leave_type_id),
    CONSTRAINT fk_aus_bal_type FOREIGN KEY (company_id, leave_type_id) REFERENCES empleado_tipo_ausencia (company_id, leave_type_id),
    CONSTRAINT `fk_aus_bal_updated_by` FOREIGN KEY (`company_id`, `updated_by`) REFERENCES `empresa_usuario` (`company_id`, `user_name`) ON DELETE RESTRICT ON UPDATE CASCADE,
    CONSTRAINT `fk_aus_bal_created_by` FOREIGN KEY (`company_id`, `created_by`) REFERENCES `empresa_usuario` (`company_id`, `user_name`) ON DELETE RESTRICT ON UPDATE CASCADE
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci ROW_FORMAT=DYNAMIC

-- ── 4. Migrate empleado_vacaciones → empleado_ausencia_balance ───────────────
-- Type 1 (DIA): days fields
INSERT INTO empleado_ausencia_balance (company_id, employee_id, `year`, leave_type_id, asignadas, ajustadas, escogidas, disponibles, schedule_id)
SELECT company_id, employee_id, `year`, 1,
       dias_asignados, dias_ajustados, dias_escogidos, dias_disponibles, schedule_id
FROM empleado_vacaciones
ON DUPLICATE KEY UPDATE asignadas = VALUES(asignadas)

-- Type 2 (HORA): hours fields
INSERT INTO empleado_ausencia_balance (company_id, employee_id, `year`, leave_type_id, asignadas, ajustadas, escogidas, disponibles, schedule_id)
SELECT company_id, employee_id, `year`, 2,
       horas_asignadas, horas_ajustadas, horas_escogidas, horas_disponibles, NULL
FROM empleado_vacaciones
WHERE horas_asignadas > 0 OR horas_escogidas > 0 OR horas_ajustadas != 0
ON DUPLICATE KEY UPDATE asignadas = VALUES(asignadas)

-- ── 5. Create empleado_ausencia_peticion ─────────────────────────────────────

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS empleado_ausencia_peticion (
    company_id          INT NOT NULL,
    employee_id         INT NOT NULL,
    `year`              INT NOT NULL,
    leave_type_id       INT NOT NULL,
    request_id          INT NOT NULL,
    fecha_inicio        DATE NOT NULL,
    fecha_fin           DATE,
    media_inicio        TINYINT(1) NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    media_fin           TINYINT(1) NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    hora_desde          TIME,
    hora_hasta          TIME,
    cantidad_efectiva   DECIMAL(10,2) NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    comentarios         TEXT NOT NULL,
    approval_status     VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'PENDING',
    approved_by         VARCHAR(45) CHARACTER SET utf8mb4 COLLATE utf8mb4_unicode_ci DEFAULT NULL,
    approved_at         DATETIME,
    rejection_reason    TEXT,
    `updated_at`        TIMESTAMP NULL DEFAULT NULL,
    `updated_by`        VARCHAR(45) CHARACTER SET utf8mb4 COLLATE utf8mb4_unicode_ci DEFAULT NULL,
    `created_at`        TIMESTAMP NULL DEFAULT NULL,
    `created_by`        VARCHAR(45) CHARACTER SET utf8mb4 COLLATE utf8mb4_unicode_ci DEFAULT NULL,
    PRIMARY KEY (company_id, employee_id, `year`, leave_type_id, request_id),
    KEY idx_aus_pet_auto (request_id),
    KEY idx_aus_pet_balance (company_id, employee_id, `year`, leave_type_id),
    CONSTRAINT `fk_aus_pet_approved_by` FOREIGN KEY (`company_id`, `approved_by`) REFERENCES `empresa_usuario` (`company_id`, `user_name`) ON DELETE RESTRICT ON UPDATE CASCADE,
    CONSTRAINT `fk_aus_pet_updated_by` FOREIGN KEY (`company_id`, `updated_by`) REFERENCES `empresa_usuario` (`company_id`, `user_name`) ON DELETE RESTRICT ON UPDATE CASCADE,
    CONSTRAINT `fk_aus_pet_created_by` FOREIGN KEY (`company_id`, `created_by`) REFERENCES `empresa_usuario` (`company_id`, `user_name`) ON DELETE RESTRICT ON UPDATE CASCADE,
    CONSTRAINT fk_aus_pet_balance FOREIGN KEY (company_id, employee_id, `year`, leave_type_id)
        REFERENCES empleado_ausencia_balance (company_id, employee_id, `year`, leave_type_id) ON DELETE CASCADE
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci ROW_FORMAT=DYNAMIC

-- ── 6. Migrate empleado_vacaciones_escogidas → empleado_ausencia_peticion (type_id=1) ──

INSERT INTO empleado_ausencia_peticion (company_id, employee_id, `year`, leave_type_id, request_id,
    fecha_inicio, fecha_fin, media_inicio, media_fin, cantidad_efectiva, comentarios,
    approval_status, approved_by, approved_at, rejection_reason)
SELECT company_id, employee_id, `year`, 1,
       ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY company_id, employee_id, `year` ORDER BY leave_day),
       leave_day,
       CASE WHEN entry_day IS NOT NULL AND entry_day != leave_day THEN entry_day ELSE NULL END,
       COALESCE(leave_half, 0),
       COALESCE(entry_half, 0),
       COALESCE(effective_days, 0),
       COALESCE(comentarios, ''),
       COALESCE(approval_status, 'PENDING'),
       approved_by,
       approved_at,
       rejection_reason
FROM empleado_vacaciones_escogidas

-- ── 7. Migrate empleado_horas_escogidas → empleado_ausencia_peticion (type_id=2) ───────

INSERT INTO empleado_ausencia_peticion (company_id, employee_id, `year`, leave_type_id, request_id,
    fecha_inicio, fecha_fin, media_inicio, media_fin, hora_desde, hora_hasta,
    cantidad_efectiva, comentarios,
    approval_status, approved_by, approved_at, rejection_reason)
SELECT company_id, employee_id, `year`, 2,
       ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY company_id, employee_id, `year` ORDER BY fecha),
       fecha,
       NULL,
       0,
       0,
       desde,
       hasta,
       COALESCE(horas, 0),
       COALESCE(descripcion, ''),
       COALESCE(approval_status, 'PENDING'),
       approved_by,
       approved_at,
       rejection_reason
FROM empleado_horas_escogidas

-- ── 8. Create empleado_ausencia_ajuste ───────────────────────────────────────

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS empleado_ausencia_ajuste (
    company_id      INT NOT NULL,
    employee_id     INT NOT NULL,
    `year`          INT NOT NULL,
    leave_type_id   INT NOT NULL,
    adjust_id       INT NOT NULL,
    ajuste          DECIMAL(10,2) NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    comentarios     TEXT NOT NULL,
    `updated_at`    TIMESTAMP NULL DEFAULT NULL,
    `updated_by`    VARCHAR(45) CHARACTER SET utf8mb4 COLLATE utf8mb4_unicode_ci DEFAULT NULL,
    `created_at`    TIMESTAMP NULL DEFAULT NULL,
    `created_by`    VARCHAR(45) CHARACTER SET utf8mb4 COLLATE utf8mb4_unicode_ci DEFAULT NULL,
    PRIMARY KEY (company_id, employee_id, `year`, leave_type_id, adjust_id),
    KEY idx_aus_aju_auto (adjust_id),
    KEY idx_aus_aju_balance (company_id, employee_id, `year`, leave_type_id),
    CONSTRAINT `fk_aus_aju_updated_by` FOREIGN KEY (`company_id`, `updated_by`) REFERENCES `empresa_usuario` (`company_id`, `user_name`) ON DELETE RESTRICT ON UPDATE CASCADE,
    CONSTRAINT `fk_aus_aju_created_by` FOREIGN KEY (`company_id`, `created_by`) REFERENCES `empresa_usuario` (`company_id`, `user_name`) ON DELETE RESTRICT ON UPDATE CASCADE,
    CONSTRAINT fk_aus_aju_balance FOREIGN KEY (company_id, employee_id, `year`, leave_type_id)
        REFERENCES empleado_ausencia_balance (company_id, employee_id, `year`, leave_type_id) ON DELETE CASCADE
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci ROW_FORMAT=DYNAMIC

-- ── 9. Migrate empleado_vacaciones_ajustes → empleado_ausencia_ajuste (type_id=1) ─

INSERT INTO empleado_ausencia_ajuste (company_id, employee_id, `year`, leave_type_id, adjust_id, ajuste, comentarios)
SELECT company_id, employee_id, `year`, 1,
       ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY company_id, employee_id, `year` ORDER BY ajuste),
       ajuste, COALESCE(comentarios, '')
FROM empleado_vacaciones_ajustes

-- ── 10. Migrate empleado_horas_ajustadas → empleado_ausencia_ajuste (type_id=2) ───

INSERT INTO empleado_ausencia_ajuste (company_id, employee_id, `year`, leave_type_id, adjust_id, ajuste, comentarios)
SELECT company_id, employee_id, `year`, 2,
       ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY company_id, employee_id, `year` ORDER BY ajuste),
       ajuste, COALESCE(comentarios, '')
FROM empleado_horas_ajustadas

-- ── 11. Create empleado_ausencia_audit_log ───────────────────────────────────

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS empleado_ausencia_audit_log (
    company_id          INT NOT NULL,
    audit_id            INT NOT NULL,
    leave_type_id       INT NOT NULL,
    request_id          INT NOT NULL,
    employee_id         INT NOT NULL,
    nombre_empleado     VARCHAR(200),
    `year`              INT NOT NULL,
    estado_anterior     VARCHAR(20),
    nuevo_estado        VARCHAR(20),
    motivo_rechazo      TEXT,
    `created_by`        VARCHAR(45) CHARACTER SET utf8mb4 COLLATE utf8mb4_unicode_ci DEFAULT NULL,
    `created_at`        TIMESTAMP NULL DEFAULT NULL,
    PRIMARY KEY (company_id, audit_id),
    KEY idx_aus_audit_auto (audit_id),
    KEY idx_aus_audit_employee (company_id, employee_id, `year`),
    KEY idx_aus_audit_type (company_id, leave_type_id),
    CONSTRAINT `fk_aus_audit_created_by` FOREIGN KEY (`company_id`, `created_by`) REFERENCES `empresa_usuario` (`company_id`, `user_name`) ON DELETE RESTRICT ON UPDATE CASCADE
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci ROW_FORMAT=DYNAMIC

-- ── 12. Migrate empleado_vacaciones_audit_log → empleado_ausencia_audit_log ──

INSERT INTO empleado_ausencia_audit_log (company_id, audit_id, leave_type_id, request_id,
    employee_id, nombre_empleado, `year`, estado_anterior, nuevo_estado,
    motivo_rechazo, created_by, created_at)
SELECT company_id, audit_id,
       CASE WHEN request_type = 'hours' THEN 2 ELSE 1 END,
       COALESCE(consume_id, 0),
       employee_id, employee_name, `year`,
       previous_status, new_status,
       rejection_reason, created_by, created_at
FROM empleado_vacaciones_audit_log

-- ── 13. Create empleado_bono_ausencia ────────────────────────────────────────

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS empleado_bono_ausencia (
    company_id      INT NOT NULL,
    leave_type_id   INT NOT NULL,
    `year`          INT NOT NULL,
    bonus_id        INT NOT NULL,
    cantidad        DECIMAL(10,2) NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    descripcion     TEXT,
    `created_by`    VARCHAR(45) CHARACTER SET utf8mb4 COLLATE utf8mb4_unicode_ci DEFAULT NULL,
    `created_at`    TIMESTAMP NULL DEFAULT NULL,
    PRIMARY KEY (company_id, leave_type_id, `year`, bonus_id),
    KEY idx_bono_aus_auto (bonus_id),
    CONSTRAINT fk_bono_aus_type FOREIGN KEY (company_id, leave_type_id) REFERENCES empleado_tipo_ausencia (company_id, leave_type_id),
    CONSTRAINT `fk_bono_aus_created_by` FOREIGN KEY (`company_id`, `created_by`) REFERENCES `empresa_usuario` (`company_id`, `user_name`) ON DELETE RESTRICT ON UPDATE CASCADE
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci ROW_FORMAT=DYNAMIC

-- ── 14. Migrate empresa_horas → empleado_bono_ausencia (type_id=2) ──────────

INSERT INTO empleado_bono_ausencia (company_id, leave_type_id, `year`, cantidad, descripcion)
SELECT company_id, 2, leaves_year_id, horas, COALESCE(descripcion, '')
FROM empresa_horas

archive/2026-03-13_001_attendance-system:
-- ============================================================================
-- Time & Attendance System Migration
-- ============================================================================

-- 1. Add GPS coordinates to entidad_centro
ALTER TABLE entidad_centro
  ADD COLUMN latitude DOUBLE NULL,
  ADD COLUMN longitude DOUBLE NULL

-- 2. Attendance configuration per center (1:1 with entidad_centro)
CREATE TABLE centro_fichaje (
  company_id        INT NOT NULL,
  client_id         INT NOT NULL,
  center_id         INT NOT NULL,
  geo_enabled       BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT FALSE,
  geo_radius_meters INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 200,
  wifi_enabled      BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT FALSE,
  wifi_ssids        VARCHAR(500) NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
  rfid_enabled      BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT FALSE,
  auto_close_hours  DECIMAL(4,2) NOT NULL DEFAULT 10.00,
  break_required    BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT FALSE,
  min_break_minutes INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 30,
  created_by        VARCHAR(100) NULL,
  created_at        DATETIME NULL,
  updated_by        VARCHAR(100) NULL,
  updated_at        DATETIME NULL,
  PRIMARY KEY (company_id, client_id, center_id),
  CONSTRAINT fk_centro_fichaje_centro
    FOREIGN KEY (company_id, client_id, center_id)
    REFERENCES entidad_centro (company_id, client_id, center_id) ON DELETE CASCADE
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4

-- 3. Individual punch events
CREATE TABLE fichaje (
  company_id        INT NOT NULL,
  employee_id       INT NOT NULL,
  punch_id          INT NOT NULL,
  punch_date        DATE NOT NULL,
  punch_time        DATETIME NOT NULL,
  punch_type        VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL,
  method            VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL,
  latitude          DOUBLE NULL,
  longitude         DOUBLE NULL,
  wifi_ssid         VARCHAR(100) NULL,
  sentinel_id       INT NULL,
  center_client_id  INT NOT NULL,
  center_id         INT NOT NULL,
  notes             VARCHAR(500) NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
  corrected_by      VARCHAR(100) NULL,
  original_punch_id INT NULL,
  created_by        VARCHAR(100) NULL,
  created_at        DATETIME NULL,
  updated_by        VARCHAR(100) NULL,
  updated_at        DATETIME NULL,
  PRIMARY KEY (company_id, employee_id, punch_id),
  CONSTRAINT fk_fichaje_empleado
    FOREIGN KEY (company_id, employee_id)
    REFERENCES empleado (company_id, employee_id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  CONSTRAINT fk_fichaje_centro
    FOREIGN KEY (company_id, center_client_id, center_id)
    REFERENCES entidad_centro (company_id, client_id, center_id),
  INDEX idx_fichaje_date (company_id, punch_date),
  INDEX idx_fichaje_employee_date (company_id, employee_id, punch_date)
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4

-- 4. Daily attendance summary (one per employee per day)
CREATE TABLE fichaje_resumen (
  company_id        INT NOT NULL,
  employee_id       INT NOT NULL,
  summary_date      DATE NOT NULL,
  first_clock_in    DATETIME NULL,
  last_clock_out    DATETIME NULL,
  total_work_mins   INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  total_break_mins  INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  effective_mins    INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  status            VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'ABSENT',
  is_open           BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT FALSE,
  punches           INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  created_by        VARCHAR(100) NULL,
  created_at        DATETIME NULL,
  updated_by        VARCHAR(100) NULL,
  updated_at        DATETIME NULL,
  PRIMARY KEY (company_id, employee_id, summary_date),
  CONSTRAINT fk_fichaje_resumen_empleado
    FOREIGN KEY (company_id, employee_id)
    REFERENCES empleado (company_id, employee_id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  INDEX idx_resumen_date (company_id, summary_date)
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4

archive/2026-03-19_002_acme-certificate-:
-- Store for ACME-issued TLS certificates (shared across cluster nodes)
-- Only one row per domain — upserted by whichever node issues/renews the cert

CREATE TABLE acme_certificate (
    domain VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,
    cert_pem LONGTEXT NOT NULL,
    key_pem LONGTEXT NOT NULL,
    account_key_pem LONGTEXT NOT NULL,
    domain_key_pem LONGTEXT NOT NULL,
    expires_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,
    issued_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,

)
archive/2026-03-20_001_fix-bridge-defaul:

UPDATE centinela SET bridge_port = 8082 WHERE bridge_port = 8080
UPDATE centinela SET bridge_https_port = 8445 WHERE bridge_https_port = 8443

archive/2026-03-21_001_attendance-phase2:
-- Attendance Phase 2: Shift Definitions + Smart Attendance Tracking
-- Adds shift time blocks, rotation support, punch cycle configuration,
-- auto-pause rules, and expected/deviation tracking.

-- 1. Shift time blocks (child of horario_laboral)
CREATE TABLE horario_laboral_turno (
  company_id    INT NOT NULL,
  schedule_id   INT NOT NULL,
  shift_id      INT NOT NULL,
  day_of_week   TINYINT NOT NULL COMMENT '1=MON .. 7=SUN (Java DayOfWeek.getValue())',
  start_time    TIME NOT NULL,
  end_time      TIME NOT NULL,
  rotation_week INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0 COMMENT '0=every week, 1=week1, 2=week2, etc.',
  `created_at` timestamp NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
  `created_by` varchar(45) CHARACTER SET utf8mb4 COLLATE utf8mb4_unicode_ci NOT NULL,
  `updated_at` timestamp NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
  `updated_by` varchar(45) CHARACTER SET utf8mb4 COLLATE utf8mb4_unicode_ci NOT NULL,
  PRIMARY KEY (company_id, schedule_id, shift_id),
  CONSTRAINT fk_turno_horario FOREIGN KEY (company_id, schedule_id)
    REFERENCES horario_laboral (company_id, schedule_id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  CONSTRAINT `fk_turno_updated_by` FOREIGN KEY (`company_id`, `updated_by`)
    REFERENCES `empresa_usuario` (`company_id`, `user_name`) ON DELETE RESTRICT ON UPDATE CASCADE,
  CONSTRAINT `fk_turno_created_by` FOREIGN KEY (`company_id`, `created_by`)
      REFERENCES `empresa_usuario` (`company_id`, `user_name`) ON DELETE RESTRICT ON UPDATE CASCADE,

  INDEX idx_turno_schedule_day (company_id, schedule_id, day_of_week)
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci ROW_FORMAT=DYNAMIC

-- 2. Rotation fields on horario_laboral
ALTER TABLE horario_laboral
  ADD COLUMN rotation_weeks  INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
  ADD COLUMN rotation_anchor DATE NULL

-- 3. Punch cycle + auto-pause on puesto_trabajo
ALTER TABLE puesto_trabajo
  ADD COLUMN punch_cycle              VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'IN_OUT',
  ADD COLUMN auto_pause_after_mins    INT NULL,
  ADD COLUMN auto_pause_duration_mins INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 30

-- 4. Expected + deviation on fichaje_resumen
ALTER TABLE fichaje_resumen
  ADD COLUMN expected_mins  INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  ADD COLUMN deviation_mins INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0
archive/2026-03-21_002_attendance-indexe:
-- Attendance Phase 2: Indexes, FK constraint, and CHECK constraint
-- Date: 2026-03-21

-- Index for auto-close scheduler task (queries is_open=true per company)
CREATE INDEX idx_resumen_open ON fichaje_resumen (company_id, is_open)

ALTER TABLE fichaje

  FOREIGN KEY (company_id, sentinel_id)
  REFERENCES centinela (company_id, sentinel_id)

archive/2026-03-22_001_center-role-flags:
-- Center Flags: virtual + active/inactive
-- esVirtual: marks centers with no physical location (e.g. auxiliary NIMA codes)
-- activo: soft-deactivation — inactive centers stay selectable but visually dimmed

ALTER TABLE entidad_centro
  ADD COLUMN es_virtual TINYINT(1) NOT NULL DEFAULT 0 COMMENT 'Virtual center, no physical location',
  ADD COLUMN activo     TINYINT(1) NOT NULL DEFAULT 1 COMMENT 'Inactive centers are dimmed in dropdowns but remain selectable'
-- Label stats VIEW: aggregates free_labels and printed_labels from mwm_etiqueta_rango
-- into entidad_centro_ler, enabling server-side sorting/filtering on these columns.

CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW v_entidad_centro_ler_labels AS
SELECT
    ecl.*,
    JSON_UNQUOTE(JSON_EXTRACT(ecl.descripcion_residuo, '$.es')) AS nombre_residuo,
    COALESCE(stats.free_labels, 0) AS free_labels,
    COALESCE(stats.printed_labels, 0) AS printed_labels
FROM entidad_centro_ler ecl
LEFT JOIN (
    SELECT
        company_id, own_client_id, own_center_id, codigo_ler, residue_id,
        SUM(free_labels) AS free_labels,
        SUM(last_label_id - first_label_id + 1 - free_labels) AS printed_labels
    FROM mwm_etiqueta_rango
    GROUP BY company_id, own_client_id, own_center_id, codigo_ler, residue_id
) stats ON ecl.company_id = stats.company_id
    AND ecl.client_id = stats.own_client_id
    AND ecl.center_id = stats.own_center_id
    AND ecl.codigo_ler = stats.codigo_ler
    AND ecl.residue_id = stats.residue_id

-- Developer Portal: partners, accounts, documents, and access grants.
-- Partners are organizations (companies) whose developers access the portal.
-- Accounts belong to a partner. Documents can be public or restricted to specific partners.

CREATE TABLE _developer_partner (
    partner_id   INT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
    name         VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    slug         VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    domain       VARCHAR(255) NULL,
    active       BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT TRUE,
    created_at   TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    updated_at   TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    UNIQUE KEY uk_dev_partner_slug (slug)
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4

CREATE TABLE _developer_account (
    account_id    INT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
    partner_id    INT NOT NULL,
    username      VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    email         VARCHAR(255) NULL,
    name          VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    password_hash VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    active        BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT TRUE,
    created_at    TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    updated_at    TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    UNIQUE KEY uk_dev_username (username),
    CONSTRAINT fk_dev_account_partner FOREIGN KEY (partner_id) REFERENCES _developer_partner (partner_id)
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4

CREATE TABLE _developer_document (
    document_id  INT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
    doc_id       VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    title        VARCHAR(500) NOT NULL,
    description  VARCHAR(1000) NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
    content      LONGTEXT NOT NULL,
    sort_order   INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    public       BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT TRUE,
    active       BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT TRUE,
    created_at   TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    updated_at   TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    UNIQUE KEY uk_dev_doc_id (doc_id)
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4

CREATE TABLE _developer_partner_document (
    partner_id   INT NOT NULL,
    document_id  INT NOT NULL,
    PRIMARY KEY (partner_id, document_id),
    CONSTRAINT fk_dpd_partner FOREIGN KEY (partner_id) REFERENCES _developer_partner (partner_id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    CONSTRAINT fk_dpd_document FOREIGN KEY (document_id) REFERENCES _developer_document (document_id) ON DELETE CASCADE
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4
archive/2026-03-26_002_document-versioni:
-- Document versioning: stores immutable snapshots of document state before each update.

CREATE TABLE _developer_document_version (
    version_id      INT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
    document_id     INT NOT NULL,
    version_number  INT NOT NULL,
    title           VARCHAR(500) NOT NULL,
    description     VARCHAR(1000) NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
    content         LONGTEXT NOT NULL,
    public_visible  BOOLEAN NOT NULL,
    changed_by      VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    change_reason   VARCHAR(500) NULL,
    created_at      TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    INDEX idx_ddv_document (document_id),
    UNIQUE KEY uk_ddv_doc_version (document_id, version_number),
    CONSTRAINT fk_ddv_document FOREIGN KEY (document_id) REFERENCES _developer_document (document_id) ON DELETE CASCADE
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4
archive/2026-03-26_003_document-change-s:
-- Add AI-generated change summary to document versions

archive/2026-03-27_001_api-contracts.sql:
-- API Contract system: structured contract definitions for partner integrations
-- Partners review and accept contracts before getting API credentials

CREATE TABLE _developer_api_contract (
    contract_id      INT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
    partner_id       INT NOT NULL,
    title            VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    description      TEXT,
    version          VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL DEFAULT '1.0',
    status           ENUM('DRAFT','PUBLISHED','ACCEPTED','APPROVED','REJECTED') NOT NULL DEFAULT 'DRAFT',
    terms            LONGTEXT NULL,
    spec             JSON NULL,
    accepted_at      DATETIME NULL,
    accepted_by      VARCHAR(255) NULL,
    approved_at      DATETIME NULL,
    approved_by      VARCHAR(255) NULL,
    rejected_at      DATETIME NULL,
    rejected_by      VARCHAR(255) NULL,
    rejection_reason VARCHAR(1000) NULL,
    created_at       TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    updated_at       TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    CONSTRAINT fk_contract_partner FOREIGN KEY (partner_id)
        REFERENCES _developer_partner (partner_id) ON DELETE CASCADE
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4

CREATE TABLE _developer_api_contract_doc (
    contract_id  INT NOT NULL,
    document_id  INT NOT NULL,
    sort_order   INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    PRIMARY KEY (contract_id, document_id),
    CONSTRAINT fk_cd_contract FOREIGN KEY (contract_id)
        REFERENCES _developer_api_contract (contract_id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    CONSTRAINT fk_cd_document FOREIGN KEY (document_id)
        REFERENCES _developer_document (document_id) ON DELETE CASCADE
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4

-- WebAuthn passkey credentials for passwordless authentication
-- Supports FIDO2/WebAuthn discoverable credentials (passkeys)

CREATE TABLE empresa_usuario_passkey (
    company_id       INT          NOT NULL,
    user_name        VARCHAR(45)  NOT NULL,
    passkey_id       INT          NOT NULL,
    credential_id    VARBINARY(1024) NOT NULL,
    public_key_cbor  BLOB         NOT NULL,
    aaguid           BINARY(16)   NULL,
    sign_count       BIGINT       NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    display_name     VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
    transports       VARCHAR(200) NULL,
    discoverable     TINYINT(1)   NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
    backed_up        TINYINT(1)   NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    attestation_fmt  VARCHAR(50)  NULL,
    last_used_at     DATETIME(3)  NULL,
    last_used_ip     VARCHAR(45)  NULL,
    created_at       DATETIME(3)  NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP(3),
    PRIMARY KEY (company_id, user_name, passkey_id),
    UNIQUE INDEX idx_credential_id (credential_id(255)),
    CONSTRAINT fk_passkey_usuario
        FOREIGN KEY (company_id, user_name)
        REFERENCES empresa_usuario (company_id, user_name)
        ON DELETE CASCADE
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci
archive/2026-04-01_002_fix-passkey-crede:
-- Fix credential_id uniqueness: prefix index (255) can collide on long credential IDs.
-- Replace with a SHA-256 hash column for full-length uniqueness.

ALTER TABLE empresa_usuario_passkey
    ADD COLUMN credential_id_hash BINARY(32) GENERATED ALWAYS AS (UNHEX(SHA2(credential_id, 256))) STORED
    AFTER created_at

ALTER TABLE empresa_usuario_passkey
    DROP INDEX idx_credential_id,
    ADD UNIQUE INDEX idx_credential_id_hash (credential_id_hash)
-- Add cleaning station support to locations
-- cleaning_station: marks location as a cleaning station (for BLE proximity detection)
-- extra_beacon_ids: optional additional BLE beacon MACs associated with the cleaning station (JSON string list)

ALTER TABLE entidad_centro_ubicacion
  ADD COLUMN cleaning_station TINYINT(1) NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  ADD COLUMN extra_beacon_ids VARCHAR(500) NULL
archive/2026-04-04_001_visibility-restri:
-- Visibility Restrictions v2: Simplified WHERE-based system
-- Replaces the old 5-table visibility system with 3 tables.

-- Drop old tables
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS user_assigned_center

-- Restriction definitions
CREATE TABLE visibility_restriction (
  company_id INT NOT NULL,
  restriction_id INT NOT NULL,
  entity_class VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
  where_clause VARCHAR(1000) NOT NULL,
  description VARCHAR(200),
  active TINYINT(1) NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
  created_by VARCHAR(100),
  created_at DATETIME,
  updated_by VARCHAR(100),
  updated_at DATETIME,
  PRIMARY KEY (company_id, restriction_id)
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4

-- User <-> restriction assignment
CREATE TABLE user_visibility (
  company_id INT NOT NULL,
  user_name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
  restriction_id INT NOT NULL,
  PRIMARY KEY (company_id, user_name, restriction_id),
  FOREIGN KEY (company_id, restriction_id) REFERENCES visibility_restriction(company_id, restriction_id)
    ON DELETE CASCADE
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4

-- Profile <-> restriction assignment
CREATE TABLE profile_visibility (
  company_id INT NOT NULL,
  profile_permission_id INT NOT NULL,
  restriction_id INT NOT NULL,
  PRIMARY KEY (company_id, profile_permission_id, restriction_id),
  FOREIGN KEY (company_id, restriction_id) REFERENCES visibility_restriction(company_id, restriction_id)
    ON DELETE CASCADE
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4

archive/2026-04-10_001_report-engine.sql:
-- Report Engine: DB-stored report definitions
-- Global tables (not company-scoped) — report definitions are shared templates.
-- Data queries use existing QueryBuilder which handles tenant isolation.

CREATE TABLE _report (
    report_id       INT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
    code            VARCHAR(50)  NOT NULL,
    title           VARCHAR(200) NOT NULL,
    description     TEXT,
    module          VARCHAR(50)  NOT NULL COMMENT 'Permission module (e.g. albaranes.albaranes)',
    base_entity     VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL COMMENT 'JPA entity name (e.g. Albaran)',
    report_type     ENUM('GROUPED', 'PIVOT') NOT NULL DEFAULT 'GROUPED',
    default_where   TEXT         COMMENT 'Optional pre-populated filter (SQL WHERE fragment)',
    orientation     ENUM('AUTO', 'PORTRAIT', 'LANDSCAPE') NOT NULL DEFAULT 'AUTO',
    active          BOOLEAN      NOT NULL DEFAULT TRUE,
    created_at      DATETIME     NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    updated_at      DATETIME     NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    UNIQUE KEY uk_report_code (code)
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4

CREATE TABLE _report_group_by (
    group_by_id     INT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
    report_id       INT          NOT NULL,
    parent_group_id INT          COMMENT 'Future: nested grouping (NULL = top-level)',
    group_key       VARCHAR(50)  NOT NULL COMMENT 'Identifier (e.g. by-product, by-date)',
    label           VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL COMMENT 'Display label for the user',
    group_fields    VARCHAR(500) NOT NULL COMMENT 'Comma-separated field paths for GROUP BY',
    group_label_tpl VARCHAR(500) NOT NULL COMMENT 'Template for group header (e.g. {producto.codigo} - {producto.nombre})',
    sort_field      VARCHAR(200) COMMENT 'Field path for ordering groups',
    sort_dir        VARCHAR(4)   NOT NULL DEFAULT 'ASC',
    display_order   INT          NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    CONSTRAINT fk_report_group_by_report FOREIGN KEY (report_id) REFERENCES _report(report_id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    CONSTRAINT fk_report_group_by_parent FOREIGN KEY (parent_group_id) REFERENCES _report_group_by(group_by_id) ON DELETE SET NULL
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4

CREATE TABLE _report_column (
    column_id       INT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
    report_id       INT          NOT NULL,
    column_key      VARCHAR(50)  NOT NULL COMMENT 'Identifier (e.g. fecha, neto)',
    label           VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL COMMENT 'Display label',
    field_path      VARCHAR(200) NOT NULL COMMENT 'QueryBuilder field path (e.g. lineas.neto, fecha)',
    format          ENUM('TEXT', 'DATE', 'DECIMAL', 'INTEGER', 'CURRENCY') NOT NULL DEFAULT 'TEXT',
    display_order   INT          NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    default_visible BOOLEAN      NOT NULL DEFAULT TRUE,
    CONSTRAINT fk_report_column_report FOREIGN KEY (report_id) REFERENCES _report(report_id) ON DELETE CASCADE
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4

CREATE TABLE _report_aggregation (
    aggregation_id  INT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
    report_id       INT          NOT NULL,
    agg_key         VARCHAR(50)  NOT NULL COMMENT 'Identifier (e.g. sumNeto)',
    label           VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL COMMENT 'Display label (e.g. Total Neto)',
    agg_function    ENUM('SUM', 'COUNT', 'AVG', 'MIN', 'MAX') NOT NULL,
    field_path      VARCHAR(200) NOT NULL COMMENT 'Field path to aggregate (e.g. lineas.neto)',
    count_distinct  BOOLEAN      NOT NULL DEFAULT FALSE,
    format          ENUM('DECIMAL', 'INTEGER', 'CURRENCY') NOT NULL DEFAULT 'DECIMAL',
    display_order   INT          NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    CONSTRAINT fk_report_aggregation_report FOREIGN KEY (report_id) REFERENCES _report(report_id) ON DELETE CASCADE
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4
