archive/2026-04-20_002_ticket-picture.sq:
-- Ticket Picture: sidecar entity for weighing/terminal photos.
-- Moves JPEG blobs out of ticket.metadata / ticket_descuento.metadata (base64)
-- and into MinIO via the existing PicturedEntity + S3ResourceStore machinery.
--
-- Surrogate picture_id PK because descuento_id is nullable (bruto/tara are
-- ticket-level, line photos are descuento-level). Uniqueness enforced via
-- uk_ticket_picture_slot.
--
-- Per-tenant picture_id assigned by IdGenerationService (Int, not AUTO_INCREMENT),
-- matching the codebase convention for company-scoped surrogate IDs.
--
-- ON DELETE CASCADE covers DB-side cleanup. The JPA deletion path
-- (deleteResourceImpl -> finishDeletion) triggers S3 object cleanup per row,
-- which requires deletion through the EntityManager — bulk SQL deletes would
-- leave MinIO orphans.

CREATE TABLE ticket_picture (
    company_id           INT          NOT NULL,
    ticket_picture_id    INT          NOT NULL,
    ticket_id            INT          NOT NULL,
    descuento_id         INT          NULL,
    slot                 VARCHAR(32)  NOT NULL COMMENT 'bruto | tara | line | future',
    picture_etag         VARCHAR(64)  NULL,
    picture_content_type VARCHAR(64)  NULL,
    picture_size         BIGINT       NULL,
    `created_at`         TIMESTAMP    NULL DEFAULT NULL,
    `created_by`         VARCHAR(45)  CHARACTER SET utf8mb4 COLLATE utf8mb4_unicode_ci DEFAULT NULL,
    `updated_at`         TIMESTAMP    NULL DEFAULT NULL,
    `updated_by`         VARCHAR(45)  CHARACTER SET utf8mb4 COLLATE utf8mb4_unicode_ci DEFAULT NULL,
    PRIMARY KEY (company_id, ticket_picture_id),
    UNIQUE KEY uk_ticket_picture_slot (company_id, ticket_id, descuento_id, slot),
    INDEX idx_ticket_picture_ticket (company_id, ticket_id),
    INDEX idx_ticket_picture_descuento (company_id, ticket_id, descuento_id),
    CONSTRAINT fk_ticket_picture_ticket
        FOREIGN KEY (company_id, ticket_id)
        REFERENCES ticket (company_id, ticket_id)
        ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE,
    CONSTRAINT fk_ticket_picture_descuento
        FOREIGN KEY (company_id, ticket_id, descuento_id)
        REFERENCES ticket_descuento (company_id, ticket_id, descuento_id)
        ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE,
    CONSTRAINT fk_ticket_picture_created_by
        FOREIGN KEY (company_id, created_by)
        REFERENCES empresa_usuario (company_id, user_name)
        ON DELETE RESTRICT ON UPDATE CASCADE,
    CONSTRAINT fk_ticket_picture_updated_by
        FOREIGN KEY (company_id, updated_by)
        REFERENCES empresa_usuario (company_id, user_name)
        ON DELETE RESTRICT ON UPDATE CASCADE
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci ROW_FORMAT=DYNAMIC
-- Unified label system: EmpresaEtiqueta + short codes.
--
-- Replaces the split between MwmEtiquetaRango/MwmEtiquetaGestor (manager labels)
-- and frontend getAndInc auto-generated labels. Each label gets a 4-8 char
-- short code for human-friendly lookup, plus the legacy labelId format for QR
-- and MwmContenedor PK compatibility.
--
-- Provider/owner FK model:
--   provider_client_id/provider_center_id — who gave us the range
--     (0/own center = we generated it ourselves)
--   owner_client_id/owner_center_id       — who the labels are for
--     (0/own center = our own use)
--   Invariant: provider_client_id == 0 OR owner_client_id == 0 (no relay ranges)
--
-- Short code format: [Prefix:2 letters] + [Seq:2-6 base32 chars] = 4-8 chars total.
-- Prefix auto-assigned per (company, codigo_ler, residue_id) via empresa_ler.label_prefix.
-- Sequence counter GLOBAL per (company, prefix) so scanned codes resolve unambiguously.
--
-- Per-tenant IDs via @AutoId / IdGenerationService (not AUTO_INCREMENT), matching
-- the codebase convention for company-scoped surrogate IDs on composite PKs.
--
-- Backward compatibility: old mwm_etiqueta_rango and mwm_etiqueta_gestor tables
-- remain untouched. mwm_contenedor.short_code is nullable — existing rows stay as
-- NULL and continue working.

-- 1. Prefix column on empresa_ler.
--    2-char letter-only prefix (22^2 = 484 residue types per company).
ALTER TABLE empresa_ler
    ADD COLUMN label_prefix CHAR(2) NULL,
    ADD UNIQUE INDEX uq_empresa_ler_label_prefix (company_id, label_prefix)

-- 2. Range entity — groups consecutive labels with shared provider/owner context.
CREATE TABLE empresa_etiqueta_rango (
    company_id              INT          NOT NULL,
    range_id                INT          NOT NULL,
    codigo_ler              VARCHAR(45)  NOT NULL,
    residue_id              INT          NOT NULL,
    provider_client_id      INT          NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    provider_center_id      INT          NOT NULL,
    owner_client_id         INT          NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    owner_center_id         INT          NOT NULL,
    first_label_number      BIGINT       NOT NULL,
    last_label_number       BIGINT       NOT NULL,
    total_labels            INT          NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    printed_labels          INT          NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    delivered_labels        INT          NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    used_labels             INT          NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    `created_at`            TIMESTAMP    NULL DEFAULT NULL,
    `created_by`            VARCHAR(45)  CHARACTER SET utf8mb4 COLLATE utf8mb4_unicode_ci DEFAULT NULL,
    `updated_at`            TIMESTAMP    NULL DEFAULT NULL,
    `updated_by`            VARCHAR(45)  CHARACTER SET utf8mb4 COLLATE utf8mb4_unicode_ci DEFAULT NULL,
    PRIMARY KEY (company_id, range_id),
    INDEX idx_eer_residue (company_id, codigo_ler, residue_id),
    INDEX idx_eer_provider (company_id, provider_client_id, provider_center_id),
    INDEX idx_eer_owner (company_id, owner_client_id, owner_center_id),
    CONSTRAINT fk_eer_empresa_ler
        FOREIGN KEY (company_id, codigo_ler, residue_id)
        REFERENCES empresa_ler (company_id, codigo_ler, residue_id)
        ON DELETE RESTRICT ON UPDATE CASCADE,
    CONSTRAINT fk_eer_created_by
        FOREIGN KEY (company_id, created_by)
        REFERENCES empresa_usuario (company_id, user_name)
        ON DELETE RESTRICT ON UPDATE CASCADE,
    CONSTRAINT fk_eer_updated_by
        FOREIGN KEY (company_id, updated_by)
        REFERENCES empresa_usuario (company_id, user_name)
        ON DELETE RESTRICT ON UPDATE CASCADE
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci ROW_FORMAT=DYNAMIC

-- 3. Individual label entity — one row per printable/usable label.
--    short_code is the human-friendly lookup key (4-8 chars, unique per company).
--    label_id is the legacy format used for QR content and MwmContenedor PK.
--    manager_label_number is the provider's original sequential number (external ranges).
CREATE TABLE empresa_etiqueta (
    company_id              INT          NOT NULL,
    etiqueta_id             INT          NOT NULL,
    short_code              VARCHAR(8)   NOT NULL,
    label_id                VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    range_id                INT          NOT NULL,
    manager_label_number    BIGINT       NULL,
    printed_at              DATETIME(6)  NULL,
    delivered_at            DATE         NULL,
    used_at                 DATE         NULL,
    `created_at`            TIMESTAMP    NULL DEFAULT NULL,
    `created_by`            VARCHAR(45)  CHARACTER SET utf8mb4 COLLATE utf8mb4_unicode_ci DEFAULT NULL,
    `updated_at`            TIMESTAMP    NULL DEFAULT NULL,
    `updated_by`            VARCHAR(45)  CHARACTER SET utf8mb4 COLLATE utf8mb4_unicode_ci DEFAULT NULL,
    PRIMARY KEY (company_id, etiqueta_id),
    UNIQUE INDEX uq_ee_short_code (company_id, short_code),
    INDEX idx_ee_label_id (company_id, label_id),
    INDEX idx_ee_range (company_id, range_id),
    CONSTRAINT fk_ee_range
        FOREIGN KEY (company_id, range_id)
        REFERENCES empresa_etiqueta_rango (company_id, range_id)
        ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE,
    CONSTRAINT fk_ee_created_by
        FOREIGN KEY (company_id, created_by)
        REFERENCES empresa_usuario (company_id, user_name)
        ON DELETE RESTRICT ON UPDATE CASCADE,
    CONSTRAINT fk_ee_updated_by
        FOREIGN KEY (company_id, updated_by)
        REFERENCES empresa_usuario (company_id, user_name)
        ON DELETE RESTRICT ON UPDATE CASCADE
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci ROW_FORMAT=DYNAMIC

-- 4. Short code on mwm_contenedor — populated when a container's label is found in
--    empresa_etiqueta (new containers) or backfilled via sysadmin migration (existing).
--    Nullable so legacy containers remain unaffected.
ALTER TABLE mwm_contenedor
    ADD COLUMN short_code VARCHAR(8) NULL,
    ADD INDEX idx_mwm_contenedor_sc (company_id, short_code)
archive/2026-04-23_001_label-migration-t:
-- Adds a `migrated_at` tracking column to the old `mwm_etiqueta_rango` so the
-- sysadmin label-migration tool (LabelMigrationEndpoint) can:
--   - skip already-migrated ranges on re-runs (idempotent)
--   - compute remaining count in GET /migration-status
--   - provide an audit trail for when each range was migrated into the new
--     EmpresaEtiqueta / EmpresaEtiquetaRango tables
--
-- Nullable: NULL means "not yet migrated". Old ranges never set this themselves.
-- Non-destructive to existing data.

ALTER TABLE mwm_etiqueta_rango
    ADD COLUMN migrated_at DATETIME(6) NULL,
    ADD INDEX idx_mer_migrated (company_id, migrated_at)
archive/2026-04-25_001_empresa-etiqueta-:
-- Path: docs/migrations/2026-04-25_001_empresa-etiqueta-impresion.sql
--
-- Phase 1 of the lifecycle-document refactor: turn print runs into a first-class
-- entity instead of a single `printed_at` timestamp on the label. Mirrors how
-- AMCS / Wastebits / Recyclos surface print history (one row per print job:
-- who, when, copies, residue, owner) and seeds the audit trail later phases
-- (Entrega, eventually a full LabelEvent log) will keep extending.
--
-- `printed_at` is preserved on `empresa_etiqueta` for back-compat — every new
-- print writes both `impresion_id` and `printed_at` in the same statement so
-- existing code paths reading `printed_at` keep working unchanged.

CREATE TABLE empresa_etiqueta_impresion (
    company_id      INT NOT NULL,
    impresion_id    INT NOT NULL,
    fecha           DATETIME(6) NOT NULL,
    user_name       VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    codigo_ler      VARCHAR(45) NOT NULL,
    residue_id      INT NOT NULL,
    owner_client_id INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    owner_center_id INT NOT NULL,
    copies          INT NOT NULL,
    created_by      VARCHAR(255),
    created_at      DATETIME(6),
    updated_by      VARCHAR(255),
    updated_at      DATETIME(6),
    PRIMARY KEY (company_id, impresion_id),
    INDEX idx_eei_owner (company_id, owner_client_id, owner_center_id),
    INDEX idx_eei_residue (company_id, codigo_ler, residue_id),
    INDEX idx_eei_fecha (company_id, fecha)
    -- Match the existing schema's collation explicitly. MySQL 8 defaults new
    -- tables to `utf8mb4_0900_ai_ci`, but `empresa_ler` / `entidad` /
    -- `entidad_centro` are `utf8mb4_unicode_ci`; the JOIN on `codigo_ler`
    -- (and similar) raises "Illegal mix of collations" without this.
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci
ALTER TABLE empresa_etiqueta ADD COLUMN impresion_id INT NULL
ALTER TABLE empresa_etiqueta ADD INDEX idx_ee_impresion (company_id, impresion_id)
archive/2026-04-26_001_empresa-etiqueta-:
-- Path: docs/migrations/2026-04-26_001_empresa-etiqueta-entrega.sql
--
-- Phase 3a of the lifecycle-document refactor: turn deliveries into a first-class
-- entity instead of a single `delivered_at` date on the label. This is the DI
-- (Documento de Identificación) document for outbound shipments — every delivery
-- now has an id, an operator, a date, and a label set.
--
-- `delivered_at` is preserved on `empresa_etiqueta` for back-compat — every new
-- delivery writes both `entrega_id` and `delivered_at` in the same statement so
-- existing code paths reading `delivered_at` keep working unchanged.
--
-- Collation note: explicit `utf8mb4_unicode_ci` to match the existing schema.
-- MySQL 8 defaults new tables to `utf8mb4_0900_ai_ci` and the join on
-- `codigo_ler` against `empresa_ler` (utf8mb4_unicode_ci) raises "Illegal mix
-- of collations" without it.

CREATE TABLE empresa_etiqueta_entrega (
    company_id      INT NOT NULL,
    entrega_id      INT NOT NULL,
    fecha           DATE NOT NULL,
    user_name       VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    codigo_ler      VARCHAR(45) NOT NULL,
    residue_id      INT NOT NULL,
    owner_client_id INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    owner_center_id INT NOT NULL,
    copies          INT NOT NULL,
    created_by      VARCHAR(255),
    created_at      DATETIME(6),
    updated_by      VARCHAR(255),
    updated_at      DATETIME(6),
    PRIMARY KEY (company_id, entrega_id),
    INDEX idx_eee_owner (company_id, owner_client_id, owner_center_id),
    INDEX idx_eee_residue (company_id, codigo_ler, residue_id),
    INDEX idx_eee_fecha (company_id, fecha)
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci
ALTER TABLE empresa_etiqueta ADD COLUMN entrega_id INT NULL
ALTER TABLE empresa_etiqueta ADD INDEX idx_ee_entrega (company_id, entrega_id)
archive/2026-04-26_002_empresa-etiqueta-:
-- Path: docs/migrations/2026-04-26_002_empresa-etiqueta-impresion-void.sql
--
-- Soft-void columns for `empresa_etiqueta_impresion`. A voided print run keeps
-- the row (audit trail — who issued it, when, who voided it, why) but the
-- linked labels go back to unprinted state so they can be reissued.
--
-- Voiding is blocked at the service layer when any child label has been
-- delivered; once it's out the door we want to record that void via the
-- delivery side, not the print side.

ALTER TABLE empresa_etiqueta_impresion
    ADD COLUMN voided_at  DATETIME(6) NULL,
    ADD COLUMN voided_by  VARCHAR(255) NULL,
    ADD COLUMN void_reason TEXT NULL

ALTER TABLE empresa_etiqueta_impresion
    ADD INDEX idx_eei_voided (company_id, voided_at)
-- Cliente etiquetas: reuse the generic Etiqueta system for Entidad.
-- Mirrors the recurso_etiqueta join table.
--
-- Effects:
--   1. Register 'entidad' as a valid Etiqueta.context value.
--   2. Create the entidad_etiqueta many-to-many join table.

-- 1. Module seed (no-op if already present).
INSERT IGNORE INTO _module (`module`) VALUES ('entidad')

-- 2. Join table.
CREATE TABLE entidad_etiqueta (
    company_id INT NOT NULL,
    client_id  INT NOT NULL,
    label_id   INT NOT NULL,
    PRIMARY KEY (company_id, client_id, label_id),
    CONSTRAINT fk_entetq_entidad
        FOREIGN KEY (company_id, client_id)
        REFERENCES entidad(company_id, client_id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    CONSTRAINT fk_entetq_etiqueta
        FOREIGN KEY (company_id, label_id)
        REFERENCES etiqueta(company_id, label_id) ON DELETE CASCADE
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci ROW_FORMAT=DYNAMIC
archive/2026-04-28_001_empresa-usuario-e:
-- User expiration support.
-- expires_on is the first day on which the user is considered expired:
-- login is blocked when CURDATE() >= expires_on, and a daily scheduler task
-- forcibly signs out any active sessions for expired users.
-- NULL means the user never expires.

ALTER TABLE empresa_usuario
    ADD COLUMN expires_on DATE NULL AFTER subscription
archive/2026-04-28_002_albaran-vinculabl: ALTER TABLE albaran DROP INDEX idx_albaran_vinculable_filter
ALTER TABLE albaran DROP COLUMN vinculable

ALTER TABLE albaran
    ADD COLUMN vinculable TINYINT(1)
        GENERATED ALWAYS AS (
            CASE
                WHEN invoice_id IS NULL
                    AND preinvoice_id IS NULL
                    AND sealed = 0
                    THEN 1
                ELSE 0
                END
            ) VIRTUAL NOT NULL
        AFTER autogenerar_di
ALTER TABLE albaran ADD INDEX idx_albaran_vinculable_filter (company_id, vinculable, fecha DESC)
archive/2026-04-28_003_automatizacion.sq:
-- Path: docs/migrations/2026-04-28_003_automatizacion.sql
-- One row per automation rule (entity-rules engine).
-- Composite PK (company_id, automatizacion_id) — tenant-scoped, no AUTO_INCREMENT
-- because MySQL does not support it on composite PK columns; the application
-- assigns automatizacion_id via @AutoId.

CREATE TABLE automatizacion (
    company_id        INT          NOT NULL,
    automatizacion_id INT          NOT NULL,
    entity_type       VARCHAR(64)  NOT NULL,
    enabled           TINYINT(1)   NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
    priority          INT          NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    predicate         JSON         NOT NULL,
    `action`          JSON         NOT NULL,
    created_by        VARCHAR(64)  NULL,
    created_at        DATETIME     NULL,
    updated_by        VARCHAR(64)  NULL,
    updated_at        DATETIME     NULL,
    PRIMARY KEY (company_id, automatizacion_id),
    KEY ix_automatizacion_lookup (company_id, entity_type, enabled, priority),
    CONSTRAINT fk_automatizacion_company
        FOREIGN KEY (company_id) REFERENCES empresa(company_id) ON DELETE CASCADE
)
archive/2026-04-28_004_automatizacion_na:
-- Path: docs/migrations/2026-04-28_004_automatizacion_name.sql
-- Adds a human-readable name to each automation rule. NOT NULL with empty
-- default lets the column land cleanly on existing rows; the application
-- hook (AutomatizacionHooks.validate) rejects blank names on CREATE/UPDATE.

ALTER TABLE automatizacion
    ADD COLUMN name VARCHAR(120) NOT NULL DEFAULT '' AFTER entity_type
archive/2026-04-30_001_fichaje-milliseco:
-- Path: docs/migrations/2026-04-30_001_fichaje-millisecond-precision.sql
-- Promote attendance time columns from DATETIME (whole-second) to DATETIME(3).
--
-- LocalDateTime.now() carries millisecond precision; plain DATETIME rounds half-up,
-- which can push a stored timestamp into the next second. validatePunchSequence()
-- compares the loaded predecessor.punchTime against an in-memory new punchTime,
-- so a CLOCK_IN inserted at .789ms is read back as the next whole second and
-- compares as greater than a CLOCK_OUT issued ~120ms later in the same wall-clock
-- second, breaking the chronological lookup and triggering punch-not-clocked-in.
-- Storing milliseconds removes the rounding step and the comparison is exact.

ALTER TABLE fichaje
    MODIFY COLUMN punch_time DATETIME(3) NOT NULL

ALTER TABLE fichaje_resumen
    MODIFY COLUMN first_clock_in DATETIME(3) NULL,
    MODIFY COLUMN last_clock_out DATETIME(3) NULL
archive/2026-05-01_001_automatizacion_ob:
-- Path: docs/migrations/2026-05-01_001_automatizacion_observability.sql
-- Schema for the automatizacion observability feature (§1 of the design spec).
--
-- 1. Add audit_mode flag to existing automatizacion rules (default false — no change to existing behaviour).
-- 2. Create automatizacion_stats: lifetime per-rule counters, lazy-created on first firing via UPSERT.
-- 3. Create automatizacion_log: append-only firing log, trimmed to 200 rows per non-audit rule.
--
-- NOTE: automatizacion_log.id uses no AUTO_INCREMENT because MySQL does not support it on
-- composite PK columns. The application assigns the id via @AutoId (IdGenerationService).

ALTER TABLE automatizacion
    ADD COLUMN audit_mode BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT FALSE

CREATE TABLE automatizacion_stats (
    company_id           INT          NOT NULL,
    automatizacion_id    INT          NOT NULL,
    fired_count          BIGINT       NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    error_count          BIGINT       NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    last_fired_at        DATETIME(3)  NULL,
    last_error_at        DATETIME(3)  NULL,
    last_error_message   VARCHAR(500) NULL,
    PRIMARY KEY (company_id, automatizacion_id),
    CONSTRAINT fk_stats_automatizacion
        FOREIGN KEY (company_id, automatizacion_id)
        REFERENCES automatizacion (company_id, automatizacion_id) ON DELETE CASCADE
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4

CREATE TABLE automatizacion_log (
    company_id          INT          NOT NULL,
    id                  INT          NOT NULL,
    automatizacion_id   INT          NOT NULL,
    entity_type         VARCHAR(80)  NOT NULL,
    entity_pk           VARCHAR(120) NOT NULL,
    fired_at            DATETIME(3)  NOT NULL,
    duration_ms         INT          NOT NULL,
    outcome             ENUM('MATCHED','FAILED') NOT NULL,
    actions_applied     JSON         NULL,
    error_message       VARCHAR(1000) NULL,
    PRIMARY KEY (company_id, id),
    INDEX idx_per_rule   (company_id, automatizacion_id, fired_at DESC),
    INDEX idx_per_entity (company_id, entity_type, entity_pk, fired_at DESC),
    CONSTRAINT fk_log_automatizacion
        FOREIGN KEY (company_id, automatizacion_id)
        REFERENCES automatizacion (company_id, automatizacion_id) ON DELETE CASCADE
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4
archive/2026-05-02_001_automatizacion_ac:
-- Path: docs/migrations/2026-05-02_001_automatizacion_action_log.sql
-- Schema for the automatizacion SendEmail action audit (§1 of the SendEmail action design spec).
--
-- One row per SendEmail action firing. Inserted as QUEUED when the email is handed to the
-- async sender; updated by the delivery callback to DELIVERED or FAILED.
--
-- NOTE: automatizacion_action_log.id uses no AUTO_INCREMENT because MySQL does not support it
-- on composite PK columns. The application assigns the id via @AutoId (IdGenerationService).

CREATE TABLE automatizacion_action_log (
    company_id            INT          NOT NULL,
    id                    INT          NOT NULL,
    automatizacion_id     INT          NOT NULL,
    automatizacion_log_id INT          NULL,
    action_type           VARCHAR(40)  NOT NULL,
    recipient             VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    subject               VARCHAR(500) NULL,
    queued_at             DATETIME(3)  NOT NULL,
    completed_at          DATETIME(3)  NULL,
    status                ENUM('QUEUED','DELIVERED','FAILED') NOT NULL,
    error_message         VARCHAR(1000) NULL,
    PRIMARY KEY (company_id, id),
    INDEX idx_per_rule  (company_id, automatizacion_id, queued_at DESC),
    INDEX idx_per_log   (company_id, automatizacion_log_id),
    -- idx_stale is intentionally not tenant-scoped: the trim task sweeps stale QUEUED rows
    -- across all tenants in a single pass. Per-tenant queries should use idx_per_rule.
    INDEX idx_stale     (status, queued_at)
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4
archive/2026-05-03_001_action_log_status:
-- Path: docs/migrations/2026-05-03_001_action_log_status_enum.sql
-- Extend automatizacion_action_log.status ENUM with RATE_LIMITED and DEDUPED.
--
-- These suppression states are produced by the SendEmail safety net (rate limiter
-- + deduper). Existing QUEUED/DELIVERED/FAILED rows are unaffected — MySQL ENUM
-- value APPENDS preserve ordinal positions of prior values, so the change is
-- online-DDL-safe and backward-compatible.
--
-- NEVER insert new values in the middle: MySQL stores ENUM as ordinal indices,
-- and inserting (instead of appending) shifts the meaning of existing rows.

ALTER TABLE automatizacion_action_log
    MODIFY status ENUM('QUEUED','DELIVERED','FAILED','RATE_LIMITED','DEDUPED') NOT NULL
archive/2026-05-04_001_automatizacion_sc:
-- Path: docs/migrations/2026-05-04_001_automatizacion_scheduled_trigger.sql
-- Add scheduled-trigger fields to `automatizacion` for the half-hour scheduler iteration.
--
-- Rules currently fire only on entity save (`trigger_type='on_save'`, the default).
-- Adding `trigger_type='scheduled'` lets a rule fire on a cron schedule. Cron expressions
-- are validated at save time to require minute ∈ {0, 30} so they align with the
-- half-hour SchedulerEngine tick.
--
-- `last_evaluated_at` is updated by the ScheduledRuleEvaluatorTask each time the rule
-- successfully runs in a bucket, so a Payara restart mid-window doesn't double-fire.

ALTER TABLE automatizacion
    ADD COLUMN trigger_type ENUM('on_save','scheduled') NOT NULL DEFAULT 'on_save',
    ADD COLUMN cron_expression VARCHAR(64) NULL,
    ADD COLUMN last_evaluated_at DATETIME(3) NULL

-- Helper index for the per-tick scan: the evaluator queries
-- `WHERE trigger_type='scheduled' AND enabled=true` per tenant. Most tenants have 0
-- or 1 scheduled rules so this index keeps the half-hour scan to O(matching rows).
CREATE INDEX idx_scheduled_rules
    ON automatizacion (company_id, trigger_type, enabled)
archive/2026-05-04_002_automatizacion_mo:
-- Register `management.automations` in `_module` and pre-seed the
-- `_module_notification` kind that SendNotification fires under.
--
-- The automatizacion endpoint module was added in an earlier release without a
-- corresponding `_module` row. Since `_module_notification.module` has a FK to
-- `_module.module`, any attempt to INSERT a `modulo_notificacion` for
-- `management.automations` (e.g. SendNotification action) fails with a 1452 FK
-- violation. SchedulerModule.ensureModuleNotifications also silently no-ops on
-- the FK error.
--
-- This migration is idempotent — INSERT IGNORE skips rows already present so
-- it's safe to re-run.

INSERT IGNORE INTO _module (`module`, actions)
VALUES ('management.automations',
        '["get","list","modify","query","delete","create","web-access"]')

INSERT IGNORE INTO _module_notification
    (`module`, notification, descripcion, tipo, send_mail, auto_subscription)
VALUES ('management.automations', 'rule_fired',
        'An automation rule fired and produced an in-app notification',
        'alert', 0, 0)
archive/2026-05-05_001_factura_prefactur:
-- 2026-05-05_001_factura_prefactura_automatizacion_fk.sql
--
-- Trazabilidad de los documentos creados por reglas de automatización.
--
-- Añade `automatizacion_id` (FK opcional → `automatizacion`) sobre `factura` y
-- `prefactura`. La acción `LinkSourcesIntoTarget` la rellena al crear el
-- documento; el editor de reglas la lee para mostrar el chip "Automatizado ·
-- {nombre regla}" en el detalle del documento, y para advertir al admin antes
-- de borrar una regla cuando hay documentos vinculados.
--
-- ON DELETE NO ACTION (RESTRICT-equivalent): MySQL no admite SET NULL en una
-- columna parcial de un FK compuesto, y `company_id` es NOT NULL en factura/
-- prefactura. Por tanto, borrar una regla con documentos vinculados queda
-- bloqueado por la BD — el editor de reglas debe nullear `automatizacion_id`
-- en los documentos antes de borrar la regla (mismo patrón que cualquier
-- "borrar entidad con FK pendiente"). Una opción de UI cómoda: el diálogo de
-- confirmación de borrado puede ofrecer "Desvincular X facturas y borrar la
-- regla" que ejecuta el null-out + delete dentro de una sola transacción.

ALTER TABLE factura
    ADD COLUMN automatizacion_id INT NULL,
    ADD CONSTRAINT fk_factura_automatizacion
        FOREIGN KEY (company_id, automatizacion_id)
        REFERENCES automatizacion (company_id, automatizacion_id)
        ON DELETE NO ACTION ON UPDATE CASCADE

CREATE INDEX idx_factura_automatizacion
    ON factura (company_id, automatizacion_id)

ALTER TABLE prefactura
    ADD COLUMN automatizacion_id INT NULL,
    ADD CONSTRAINT fk_prefactura_automatizacion
        FOREIGN KEY (company_id, automatizacion_id)
        REFERENCES automatizacion (company_id, automatizacion_id)
        ON DELETE NO ACTION ON UPDATE CASCADE

CREATE INDEX idx_prefactura_automatizacion
    ON prefactura (company_id, automatizacion_id)
archive/2026-05-06_001_tracking-m1-schem:
-- 2026-05-06_001_tracking-m1-schema.sql
--
-- Tracking system — Milestone 1 schema. Spec v2.4.4 §3.1 + §3.2.
--
-- Touches:
--   * vehiculo                  — ALTER: add live-state columns + FK to tracking_device
--                                 (the table itself already exists; PK column is
--                                 vehicle_id from the original schema, not a new column)
--   * tracking_device           — CREATE: physical GPS device, IMEI globally unique
--   * ingest_record_dedup       — CREATE: cross-store idempotency (CLAIMED → MONGO_WRITTEN → ERROR)
--   * tracking_job              — CREATE: async work queue (PENDING/RUNNING/DONE/DEAD)
--   * tracking_processor_cursor — CREATE: per-device per-kind worker cursor for restart safety
--
-- M2 will add: trip, trip_position_range, trip_stop, alert, alert_rule,
-- alert_rule_state, geofence, trip_audit_finding, tracking_device_sms_log.
-- Those are NOT in this migration to keep M1 focused. Forward-looking
-- live-state columns the workers will need (last_altitude, current_speed,
-- current_heading, state_since, current_trip_id, tachograph) are also
-- deferred — M1 ingest only writes the columns added below.
--
-- Conventions:
--   * Composite PKs lead with company_id (CompanyPK pattern).
--   * PK columns use English names (vehicle_id, tracking_device_id) — see
--     existing schema and the project's PK-naming convention.
--   * NEVER use AUTO_INCREMENT on composite-PK ID columns — the app generates
--     IDs via @AutoId. Single-column AUTO_INCREMENT PKs (tracking_job.job_id)
--     are fine since they don't conflict with the @AutoId mechanism.
--   * Audit columns: created_by VARCHAR(255), created_at DATETIME(6),
--     updated_by VARCHAR(255), updated_at DATETIME(6) — millisecond+ precision
--     to match other ZnEntity tables in this codebase.
--   * BOOLEAN with explicit NOT NULL DEFAULT.

-- =============================================================================
-- 1. vehiculo — extend the existing canonical truck/vehicle record
-- =============================================================================
-- The vehiculo table already exists in production with the original fleet
-- columns (matricula, vin, marca, modelo, year, tara, mma, tacografo
-- (LocalDate), seguro_*, recursos, servicios, etc.). This migration adds
-- the tracking live-state columns the four-phase ingest's Phase 4 writes.
-- The FK to tracking_device is added after that table is created below.
ALTER TABLE vehiculo
    ADD COLUMN last_lat                  DECIMAL(11,7) NULL,
    ADD COLUMN last_lng                  DECIMAL(11,7) NULL,
    ADD COLUMN last_location_ts          DATETIME(6)   NULL,
    ADD COLUMN last_seen                 DATETIME(6)   NULL,
    ADD COLUMN moving                    BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT FALSE,
    ADD COLUMN ignition                  BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT FALSE,
    -- Newest deviceTs ever processed for this vehicle. The Phase-4 idempotent
    -- UPDATE is guarded by `last_ingested_device_ts < :ts` so a stale retry
    -- silently no-ops rather than overwriting more recent state. Spec §4.2.
    ADD COLUMN last_ingested_device_ts   DATETIME(6)   NULL,
    -- 1:1 device pointer; FK declared after tracking_device exists below.
    ADD COLUMN device_id                 INT           NULL,
    ADD KEY idx_vehiculo_last_seen (company_id, last_seen),
    ADD KEY idx_vehiculo_device    (company_id, device_id)

-- =============================================================================
-- 2. tracking_device — physical hardware
-- =============================================================================
CREATE TABLE tracking_device (
    company_id                  INT NOT NULL,
    tracking_device_id          INT NOT NULL,

    -- IMEI is GLOBALLY unique (not within company). Devices send only their
    -- IMEI on connect; tenant must be resolvable from IMEI alone. Demo IMEIs
    -- use a "DEMO-" prefix to remain unambiguous from real 15-digit IMEIs.
    -- Spec §3.1.
    imei                        VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL,

    model                       VARCHAR(32) NULL,
    enabled                     BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT TRUE,

    -- Optional Teltonika "authentication string". Encrypted at rest with
    -- crypto V2 envelope. See spec §11 / §15.5.
    device_secret_encrypted     VARBINARY(512) NULL,

    match_vin                   BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT FALSE,
    firmware                    VARCHAR(64) NULL,
    last_seen                   DATETIME(6) NULL,
    last_connect                DATETIME(6) NULL,

    -- SMS configuration (M6 fills these in via SMS provisioning workflows).
    -- Columns added now to avoid a second migration touching the same table.
    sim_phone_number            VARCHAR(20) NULL,
    sim_iccid                   VARCHAR(32) NULL,
    sms_password_encrypted      VARBINARY(512) NULL,
    sms_last_command            VARCHAR(255) NULL,
    sms_last_command_at         DATETIME(6) NULL,
    sms_last_response           VARCHAR(2048) NULL,
    sms_last_response_at        DATETIME(6) NULL,
    sms_config_state            VARCHAR(32) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'UNCONFIGURED',

    created_by                  VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    created_at                  DATETIME(6) NOT NULL,
    updated_by                  VARCHAR(255) NULL,
    updated_at                  DATETIME(6) NULL,

    PRIMARY KEY (company_id, tracking_device_id),
    UNIQUE KEY uq_tracking_device_imei (imei)
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci

-- Now that tracking_device exists, add the FK from vehiculo.
-- RESTRICT (not SET NULL): the FK includes company_id (NOT NULL, tenant PK), so
-- ON DELETE SET NULL is illegal (MySQL 1830). Unlink the device from the vehicle
-- (clear vehiculo.device_id) before deleting a tracking_device. Matches fk_tpr_device.
ALTER TABLE vehiculo
    ADD CONSTRAINT fk_vehiculo_device
        FOREIGN KEY (company_id, device_id)
        REFERENCES tracking_device (company_id, tracking_device_id)
        ON DELETE RESTRICT ON UPDATE CASCADE

-- =============================================================================
-- 3. ingest_record_dedup — cross-store idempotency table
-- =============================================================================
-- Spec §3.2 + §4.2. The four-phase ingest commits CLAIMED rows in Phase 1
-- (REQUIRES_NEW), writes to Mongo in Phase 2, transitions CLAIMED →
-- MONGO_WRITTEN in Phase 3 (REQUIRES_NEW), then runs Phase 4 idempotent
-- post-ingest. The recovery sweep (Task 1.24) re-drives orphans.
--
-- canonical_record_json holds the parsed record while state='CLAIMED' so the
-- recovery sweep can reconstruct the Mongo write without depending on the
-- gateway still retrying. NULLed on transition to MONGO_WRITTEN to bound
-- storage.
CREATE TABLE ingest_record_dedup (
    company_id              INT NOT NULL,
    device_id               INT NOT NULL,
    device_ts               DATETIME(6) NOT NULL,
    record_key              CHAR(64) NOT NULL,                 -- SHA-256 hex from gateway

    source_batch_ulid       CHAR(26) NOT NULL,
    source_record_index     INT NOT NULL,

    canonical_record_json   LONGTEXT NULL,                     -- non-NULL while CLAIMED

    -- CLAIMED | MONGO_WRITTEN | ERROR (NO 'DONE' — that was a v2.2 alias removed in v2.3)
    state                   VARCHAR(16) NOT NULL,

    first_seen_at           DATETIME(6) NOT NULL,
    updated_at              DATETIME(6) NOT NULL,
    expires_at              DATETIME(6) NOT NULL,
    error_message           VARCHAR(1024) NULL,

    PRIMARY KEY (company_id, device_id, device_ts, record_key),
    KEY idx_dedup_state_updated (state, updated_at),           -- recovery sweep
    KEY idx_dedup_expires (expires_at),                        -- daily purge
    CONSTRAINT fk_dedup_device
        FOREIGN KEY (company_id, device_id)
        REFERENCES tracking_device (company_id, tracking_device_id)
        ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci

-- =============================================================================
-- 4. tracking_job — async work queue
-- =============================================================================
-- Spec §3.2. Atomic claim with locked_until for stale-claim recovery. Single-
-- column AUTO_INCREMENT PK — does not conflict with @AutoId since this is not
-- a composite PK.
--
-- States:
--   PENDING — eligible for claim once available_at <= NOW()
--   RUNNING — currently leased by claimed_by until locked_until
--   DONE    — completed successfully (terminal; pruned by retention)
--   DEAD    — terminal failure or transient retries exhausted; operator-retryable
-- (NO separate FAILED state — the v2.3 vocabulary cleanup unified that as DEAD.)
--
-- Selection query uses (available_at, state, priority, job_id) and includes
-- expired-lease RUNNING rows to reclaim from dead nodes. See spec §3.2.
CREATE TABLE tracking_job (
    job_id              BIGINT NOT NULL AUTO_INCREMENT,
    company_id          INT NOT NULL,
    kind                VARCHAR(32) NOT NULL,
    payload             LONGTEXT NOT NULL,
    state               VARCHAR(16) NOT NULL,
    priority            INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    attempts            INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    max_attempts        INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 5,
    available_at        DATETIME(6) NOT NULL,
    locked_until        DATETIME(6) NULL,
    idempotency_key     VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    claimed_by          VARCHAR(64) NULL,
    claimed_at          DATETIME(6) NULL,
    last_error          VARCHAR(2048) NULL,
    error_class         VARCHAR(64) NULL,
    created_at          DATETIME(6) NOT NULL,
    updated_at          DATETIME(6) NOT NULL,

    PRIMARY KEY (job_id),
    UNIQUE KEY uq_tracking_job_idempotency (idempotency_key),
    KEY idx_tracking_job_selection (available_at, state, priority, job_id),
    KEY idx_tracking_job_company_state (company_id, state)
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci

-- =============================================================================
-- 5. tracking_processor_cursor — per-device per-kind worker progress
-- =============================================================================
-- Spec §3.2. Workers (TRIP_DELTA, ALERT_EVAL — both M2) read records strictly
-- greater than (last_processed_ts, last_processed_batch_ulid,
-- last_processed_record_index). last_processed_record_index is the v2.3 fix
-- for breaking ties when multiple records share the same device timestamp
-- within a single gateway batch.
--
-- Created in M1 even though the workers that USE it are M2, because the
-- recovery sweep advances cursors when re-driving orphan batches.
CREATE TABLE tracking_processor_cursor (
    company_id                      INT NOT NULL,
    device_id                       INT NOT NULL,
    kind                            VARCHAR(32) NOT NULL,
    last_processed_ts               DATETIME(6) NULL,
    last_processed_batch_ulid       CHAR(26) NULL,
    last_processed_record_index     INT NULL,
    last_processed_at               DATETIME(6) NOT NULL,

    PRIMARY KEY (company_id, device_id, kind),
    CONSTRAINT fk_cursor_device
        FOREIGN KEY (company_id, device_id)
        REFERENCES tracking_device (company_id, tracking_device_id)
        ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci
archive/2026-05-07_001_multitrx-test-row:
-- Test table for the multiTrx framework primitive's load harness + e2e tests.
-- Tenant-scoped (`(company_id, row_id)`). Not a production schema — exists
-- solely so the harness has somewhere to insert.
--
-- Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-07-multitrx-framework-primitive-design.md
--
-- Apply locally:
--   mysql -u root zincdb < docs/migrations/2026-05-07_001_multitrx-test-row.sql
--
-- After applying locally, MOVE this file to docs/migrations/already_run/
-- per the project convention.

CREATE TABLE multi_trx_test_row (
    company_id INT          NOT NULL,
    row_id     INT          NOT NULL,
    label      VARCHAR(64)  NOT NULL,
    counter    INT          NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    created_at TIMESTAMP(3) NULL,
    created_by VARCHAR(64)  NULL,
    updated_at TIMESTAMP(3) NULL,
    updated_by VARCHAR(64)  NULL,
    PRIMARY KEY (company_id, row_id),
    KEY idx_multi_trx_test_row_label (company_id, label)
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci
archive/2026-05-08_001_vehiculo-live-hea:
-- =============================================================================
-- Tracking M3 — extend vehiculo with heading + speed live-state columns.
--
-- Phase 4 ingest will write these alongside the M1 live-state columns so the
-- frontend live-tracking page can render heading-arrow markers and show
-- current speed in the side panel without round-tripping to Mongo.
--
-- Conventions match docs/migrations/already_run/2026-05-06_001_tracking-m1-schema.sql:
--   * Composite-PK / tenant-scoped table — additive ALTER, no AUTO_INCREMENT
--   * Columns are nullable so the migration is safe on existing rows
--   * SMALLINT range covers 0..359 (heading), DECIMAL(5,2) covers 0.00..999.99 km/h
-- =============================================================================

ALTER TABLE vehiculo
    ADD COLUMN last_heading SMALLINT NULL COMMENT 'Heading in degrees 0-359, latest reading from Phase 4',
    ADD COLUMN last_speed   DECIMAL(5,2) NULL COMMENT 'Speed in km/h, latest reading from Phase 4'
archive/2026-05-09_001_demo-tenant-track:
-- =============================================================================
-- Demo tenant fleet for the mock-truck simulator.
--
-- Seeds 9 synthetic vehicles + 9 tracking_device rows on companyId=2 (the
-- demo tenant). Vehicles use the 999...91-99 IMEI range so they're visually
-- distinct from real-fleet IMEIs (e.g., the live truck 353742374514669) and
-- carry marca='Mock' so sales-facing UI labels them unambiguously.
--
-- DGT vehicle types used:
--   12 = Camión, 14 = Furgoneta, 10 = Turismo
--
-- Audit columns (created_by/updated_by) use '_sysadmin' because the
-- vehiculo table has FK constraints to empresa_usuario; '_sysadmin' is the
-- guaranteed system user present on every tenant. Mock provenance is
-- carried via marca='Mock' (vehiculo) and model='FMC920-MOCK'
-- (tracking_device) instead.
--
-- This migration is NOT idempotent (the IMEI UNIQUE constraint will reject
-- re-runs). To re-seed, run the cleanup first:
--
--   DELETE FROM vehiculo
--    WHERE company_id = 2
--      AND matricula IN ('3142BCD','4892FGH','5103JKL','2847MNP','6321RST',
--                        '7459BFC','8612DGH','1925JKL','3076MNP');
--   DELETE FROM tracking_device
--    WHERE imei BETWEEN '999999999999991' AND '999999999999999';
--
-- Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-08-mock-truck-simulator-design.md §9.1
-- =============================================================================

SET @next_device_id = (SELECT COALESCE(MAX(tracking_device_id), 0) + 1
                         FROM tracking_device WHERE company_id = 2)

INSERT INTO tracking_device (company_id, tracking_device_id, imei, model, enabled,
                              match_vin, sms_config_state, created_by, created_at)
VALUES
  (2, @next_device_id + 0, '999999999999991', 'FMC920-MOCK', TRUE, FALSE, 'UNCONFIGURED', '_sysadmin', NOW(6)),
  (2, @next_device_id + 1, '999999999999992', 'FMC920-MOCK', TRUE, FALSE, 'UNCONFIGURED', '_sysadmin', NOW(6)),
  (2, @next_device_id + 2, '999999999999993', 'FMC920-MOCK', TRUE, FALSE, 'UNCONFIGURED', '_sysadmin', NOW(6)),
  (2, @next_device_id + 3, '999999999999994', 'FMC920-MOCK', TRUE, FALSE, 'UNCONFIGURED', '_sysadmin', NOW(6)),
  (2, @next_device_id + 4, '999999999999995', 'FMC920-MOCK', TRUE, FALSE, 'UNCONFIGURED', '_sysadmin', NOW(6)),
  (2, @next_device_id + 5, '999999999999996', 'FMC920-MOCK', TRUE, FALSE, 'UNCONFIGURED', '_sysadmin', NOW(6)),
  (2, @next_device_id + 6, '999999999999997', 'FMC920-MOCK', TRUE, FALSE, 'UNCONFIGURED', '_sysadmin', NOW(6)),
  (2, @next_device_id + 7, '999999999999998', 'FMC920-MOCK', TRUE, FALSE, 'UNCONFIGURED', '_sysadmin', NOW(6)),
  (2, @next_device_id + 8, '999999999999999', 'FMC920-MOCK', TRUE, FALSE, 'UNCONFIGURED', '_sysadmin', NOW(6))

SET @next_vehicle_id = (SELECT COALESCE(MAX(vehicle_id), 0) + 1
                          FROM vehiculo WHERE company_id = 2)

INSERT INTO vehiculo (company_id, vehicle_id, matricula, marca, modelo,
                       vehicle_type_id, device_id,
                       created_by, created_at, updated_by, updated_at,
                       seguro_aseguradora, seguro_poliza, seguro_comentarios, seguro_precio)
VALUES
  (2, @next_vehicle_id + 0, '3142BCD', 'Mock', 'Mock-Camion',     12, @next_device_id + 0, '_sysadmin', NOW(6), '_sysadmin', NOW(6), '', '', '', 0),
  (2, @next_vehicle_id + 1, '4892FGH', 'Mock', 'Mock-Camion',     12, @next_device_id + 1, '_sysadmin', NOW(6), '_sysadmin', NOW(6), '', '', '', 0),
  (2, @next_vehicle_id + 2, '5103JKL', 'Mock', 'Mock-Camion',     12, @next_device_id + 2, '_sysadmin', NOW(6), '_sysadmin', NOW(6), '', '', '', 0),
  (2, @next_vehicle_id + 3, '2847MNP', 'Mock', 'Mock-Camion',     12, @next_device_id + 3, '_sysadmin', NOW(6), '_sysadmin', NOW(6), '', '', '', 0),
  (2, @next_vehicle_id + 4, '6321RST', 'Mock', 'Mock-Camion',     12, @next_device_id + 4, '_sysadmin', NOW(6), '_sysadmin', NOW(6), '', '', '', 0),
  (2, @next_vehicle_id + 5, '7459BFC', 'Mock', 'Mock-Furgoneta',  14, @next_device_id + 5, '_sysadmin', NOW(6), '_sysadmin', NOW(6), '', '', '', 0),
  (2, @next_vehicle_id + 6, '8612DGH', 'Mock', 'Mock-Furgoneta',  14, @next_device_id + 6, '_sysadmin', NOW(6), '_sysadmin', NOW(6), '', '', '', 0),
  (2, @next_vehicle_id + 7, '1925JKL', 'Mock', 'Mock-Turismo',    10, @next_device_id + 7, '_sysadmin', NOW(6), '_sysadmin', NOW(6), '', '', '', 0),
  (2, @next_vehicle_id + 8, '3076MNP', 'Mock', 'Mock-Turismo',    10, @next_device_id + 8, '_sysadmin', NOW(6), '_sysadmin', NOW(6), '', '', '', 0)
