archive/2026-05-10_001_recurso-nt-id.sql:
-- Add nt_id column to _recurso for resources attached to NotificacionPrevia.
-- Replaces the metadata['ntId'] JSON-field workaround used by the La Paz importer.
-- The composite FK is (company_id, nt_id), matching NotificacionPrevia's PK.

ALTER TABLE recurso
  ADD COLUMN nt_id INT NULL COMMENT 'FK to notificacion_previa.nt_id; NULL for resources not attached to an NT',
  ADD INDEX idx_recurso_nt_id (company_id, nt_id)
archive/2026-05-11_001_m2-trip-tables.sq:
-- M2 trip detection tables (spec v0.2 §3).
-- Idempotent: re-applies cleanly via CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS.
-- Requires MySQL 8.0+.
--
-- Conventions followed (verified against `albaran` / `albaran_linea`):
--  * ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci ROW_FORMAT=DYNAMIC
--  * Audit columns: created_by varchar(45) NOT NULL FK→empresa_usuario, created_at
--    timestamp NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP, same shape for updated_*.
--    Application populates _by via @UserName; DB defaults the _at via CURRENT_TIMESTAMP.
--    There is NO column-level default for _by (test inserts must supply '_sysadmin'
--    or another valid empresa_usuario.user_name explicitly).
--  * Parenting: child entities (trip_stop, trip_position_range, trip_audit_finding)
--    SHARE the parent's PK (company_id, trip_id) and add their own auto-id segment.
--    Parent FK is ON DELETE CASCADE so deleting a Trip cascades to all children.
--  * Index/constraint naming: idx_<table>_<purpose>, fk_<table>_<purpose>, uq_<table>_<purpose>.
--
-- NOTE: mutual-exclusivity of (work_order_id, albaran_id) and of
-- (candidate_work_order_id, candidate_albaran_id) is enforced at the
-- APPLICATION LAYER via JPA hooks on the Trip entity (see Task 1.2).
-- MySQL 8.0+ rejects CHECK constraints on columns that also participate in
-- a FK with referential actions (error 3823); the two columns above are FKs
-- with ON DELETE RESTRICT ON UPDATE CASCADE, so DB-layer CHECK is unavailable.
-- A direct SQL insert that bypasses JPA could violate the invariant; M2's
-- only writers go through the authoritative linker which fires hooks.

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS trip (
    company_id INT NOT NULL,
    trip_id INT NOT NULL,
    vehicle_id INT NOT NULL,

    status VARCHAR(32) CHARACTER SET utf8mb4 COLLATE utf8mb4_unicode_ci NOT NULL DEFAULT 'ACTIVE',
    started_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    ended_at TIMESTAMP NULL DEFAULT NULL,
    ended_at_lat DECIMAL(10,7) NULL DEFAULT NULL,
    ended_at_lng DECIMAL(10,7) NULL DEFAULT NULL,
    finalized_at TIMESTAMP NULL DEFAULT NULL,
    movement_started_at TIMESTAMP NULL DEFAULT NULL,
    movement_ended_at TIMESTAMP NULL DEFAULT NULL,
    finalize_generation INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,

    settled_centro_client_id INT NULL DEFAULT NULL,
    settled_centro_center_id INT NULL DEFAULT NULL,

    distance_meters BIGINT NULL DEFAULT NULL,
    max_speed_kmh DECIMAL(6,2) NULL DEFAULT NULL,
    avg_speed_kmh DECIMAL(6,2) NULL DEFAULT NULL,
    stopped_seconds BIGINT NULL DEFAULT NULL,
    position_count INT NULL DEFAULT NULL,
    gaps JSON NULL DEFAULT NULL,

    candidate_work_order_id INT NULL DEFAULT NULL,
    candidate_albaran_id INT NULL DEFAULT NULL,
    candidate_link_confidence VARCHAR(16) CHARACTER SET utf8mb4 COLLATE utf8mb4_unicode_ci NULL DEFAULT NULL,
    candidate_linked_at TIMESTAMP NULL DEFAULT NULL,

    work_order_id INT NULL DEFAULT NULL,
    albaran_id INT NULL DEFAULT NULL,
    link_confidence VARCHAR(16) CHARACTER SET utf8mb4 COLLATE utf8mb4_unicode_ci NULL DEFAULT NULL,
    linked_by VARCHAR(45) CHARACTER SET utf8mb4 COLLATE utf8mb4_unicode_ci NULL DEFAULT NULL,
    linked_at TIMESTAMP NULL DEFAULT NULL,

    created_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    created_by VARCHAR(45) CHARACTER SET utf8mb4 COLLATE utf8mb4_unicode_ci NOT NULL,
    updated_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    updated_by VARCHAR(45) CHARACTER SET utf8mb4 COLLATE utf8mb4_unicode_ci NOT NULL,

    PRIMARY KEY (company_id, trip_id) USING BTREE,

    UNIQUE KEY uq_trip_work_order (company_id, work_order_id) USING BTREE,
    UNIQUE KEY uq_trip_albaran (company_id, albaran_id) USING BTREE,

    KEY idx_trip_vehicle_started (company_id, vehicle_id, started_at DESC) USING BTREE,
    KEY idx_trip_status_ended (company_id, status, ended_at) USING BTREE,
    KEY idx_trip_created_by (company_id, created_by) USING BTREE,
    KEY idx_trip_updated_by (company_id, updated_by) USING BTREE,

    CONSTRAINT fk_trip_empresa
        FOREIGN KEY (company_id) REFERENCES empresa (company_id)
        ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE,
    CONSTRAINT fk_trip_vehiculo
        FOREIGN KEY (company_id, vehicle_id) REFERENCES vehiculo (company_id, vehicle_id)
        ON DELETE RESTRICT ON UPDATE CASCADE,
    CONSTRAINT fk_trip_work_order
        FOREIGN KEY (company_id, work_order_id) REFERENCES orden_trabajo (company_id, order_id)
        ON DELETE RESTRICT ON UPDATE CASCADE,
    CONSTRAINT fk_trip_albaran
        FOREIGN KEY (company_id, albaran_id) REFERENCES albaran (company_id, albaran_id)
        ON DELETE RESTRICT ON UPDATE CASCADE,
    CONSTRAINT fk_trip_created_by
        FOREIGN KEY (company_id, created_by) REFERENCES empresa_usuario (company_id, user_name)
        ON DELETE RESTRICT ON UPDATE CASCADE,
    CONSTRAINT fk_trip_updated_by
        FOREIGN KEY (company_id, updated_by) REFERENCES empresa_usuario (company_id, user_name)
        ON DELETE RESTRICT ON UPDATE CASCADE
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci ROW_FORMAT=DYNAMIC

-- ===========================================================================
-- trip_stop — CHILD of trip (shares (company_id, trip_id) in PK)
-- ===========================================================================
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS trip_stop (
    company_id INT NOT NULL,
    trip_id INT NOT NULL,
    trip_stop_id INT NOT NULL,

    lat DECIMAL(10,7) NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    lng DECIMAL(10,7) NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    started_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    ended_at TIMESTAMP NULL DEFAULT NULL,
    duration_seconds BIGINT NULL DEFAULT NULL,

    kind VARCHAR(32) CHARACTER SET utf8mb4 COLLATE utf8mb4_unicode_ci NOT NULL DEFAULT 'GENERAL',
    matched_centro_client_id INT NULL DEFAULT NULL,
    matched_centro_center_id INT NULL DEFAULT NULL,
    matched_via VARCHAR(32) CHARACTER SET utf8mb4 COLLATE utf8mb4_unicode_ci NULL DEFAULT NULL,

    created_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    created_by VARCHAR(45) CHARACTER SET utf8mb4 COLLATE utf8mb4_unicode_ci NOT NULL,
    updated_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    updated_by VARCHAR(45) CHARACTER SET utf8mb4 COLLATE utf8mb4_unicode_ci NOT NULL,

    PRIMARY KEY (company_id, trip_id, trip_stop_id) USING BTREE,

    KEY idx_trip_stop_trip_time (company_id, trip_id, started_at) USING BTREE,
    KEY idx_trip_stop_matched_centro (company_id, matched_centro_client_id, matched_centro_center_id) USING BTREE,
    KEY idx_trip_stop_created_by (company_id, created_by) USING BTREE,
    KEY idx_trip_stop_updated_by (company_id, updated_by) USING BTREE,

    CONSTRAINT fk_trip_stop_trip
        FOREIGN KEY (company_id, trip_id) REFERENCES trip (company_id, trip_id)
        ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE,
    CONSTRAINT fk_trip_stop_matched_centro
        FOREIGN KEY (company_id, matched_centro_client_id, matched_centro_center_id)
        REFERENCES entidad_centro (company_id, client_id, center_id)
        ON DELETE RESTRICT ON UPDATE CASCADE,
    CONSTRAINT fk_trip_stop_created_by
        FOREIGN KEY (company_id, created_by) REFERENCES empresa_usuario (company_id, user_name)
        ON DELETE RESTRICT ON UPDATE CASCADE,
    CONSTRAINT fk_trip_stop_updated_by
        FOREIGN KEY (company_id, updated_by) REFERENCES empresa_usuario (company_id, user_name)
        ON DELETE RESTRICT ON UPDATE CASCADE
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci ROW_FORMAT=DYNAMIC

-- ===========================================================================
-- trip_position_range — CHILD of trip
-- ===========================================================================
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS trip_position_range (
    company_id INT NOT NULL,
    trip_id INT NOT NULL,
    range_id INT NOT NULL,

    device_id INT NOT NULL,
    start_ts TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    end_ts TIMESTAMP NULL DEFAULT NULL,

    created_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    created_by VARCHAR(45) CHARACTER SET utf8mb4 COLLATE utf8mb4_unicode_ci NOT NULL,
    updated_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    updated_by VARCHAR(45) CHARACTER SET utf8mb4 COLLATE utf8mb4_unicode_ci NOT NULL,

    PRIMARY KEY (company_id, trip_id, range_id) USING BTREE,

    KEY idx_tpr_trip_start (company_id, trip_id, start_ts) USING BTREE,
    KEY idx_tpr_device_window (company_id, device_id, start_ts, end_ts) USING BTREE,
    KEY idx_tpr_created_by (company_id, created_by) USING BTREE,
    KEY idx_tpr_updated_by (company_id, updated_by) USING BTREE,

    CONSTRAINT fk_tpr_trip
        FOREIGN KEY (company_id, trip_id) REFERENCES trip (company_id, trip_id)
        ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE,
    CONSTRAINT fk_tpr_device
        FOREIGN KEY (company_id, device_id)
        REFERENCES tracking_device (company_id, tracking_device_id)
        ON DELETE RESTRICT ON UPDATE CASCADE,
    CONSTRAINT fk_tpr_created_by
        FOREIGN KEY (company_id, created_by) REFERENCES empresa_usuario (company_id, user_name)
        ON DELETE RESTRICT ON UPDATE CASCADE,
    CONSTRAINT fk_tpr_updated_by
        FOREIGN KEY (company_id, updated_by) REFERENCES empresa_usuario (company_id, user_name)
        ON DELETE RESTRICT ON UPDATE CASCADE
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci ROW_FORMAT=DYNAMIC

-- ===========================================================================
-- trip_audit_finding — CHILD of trip
-- ===========================================================================
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS trip_audit_finding (
    company_id INT NOT NULL,
    trip_id INT NOT NULL,
    finding_id INT NOT NULL,

    kind VARCHAR(32) CHARACTER SET utf8mb4 COLLATE utf8mb4_unicode_ci NOT NULL,
    details JSON NOT NULL,

    created_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    created_by VARCHAR(45) CHARACTER SET utf8mb4 COLLATE utf8mb4_unicode_ci NOT NULL,
    updated_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    updated_by VARCHAR(45) CHARACTER SET utf8mb4 COLLATE utf8mb4_unicode_ci NOT NULL,

    PRIMARY KEY (company_id, trip_id, finding_id) USING BTREE,

    KEY idx_taf_trip_kind (company_id, trip_id, kind) USING BTREE,
    KEY idx_taf_created_by (company_id, created_by) USING BTREE,
    KEY idx_taf_updated_by (company_id, updated_by) USING BTREE,

    CONSTRAINT fk_taf_trip
        FOREIGN KEY (company_id, trip_id) REFERENCES trip (company_id, trip_id)
        ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE,
    CONSTRAINT fk_taf_created_by
        FOREIGN KEY (company_id, created_by) REFERENCES empresa_usuario (company_id, user_name)
        ON DELETE RESTRICT ON UPDATE CASCADE,
    CONSTRAINT fk_taf_updated_by
        FOREIGN KEY (company_id, updated_by) REFERENCES empresa_usuario (company_id, user_name)
        ON DELETE RESTRICT ON UPDATE CASCADE
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci ROW_FORMAT=DYNAMIC

-- ===========================================================================
-- tracking_processor_cursor — TOP-LEVEL (per-vehicle singleton, no parent)
-- ===========================================================================
--
-- Note: an M1-era `tracking_processor_cursor` was created speculatively (PK
-- on `device_id`, not `vehicle_id`) and is empty in every environment per
-- operator confirmation. Drop it before re-creating with the M2 shape so
-- `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS` is not a silent no-op on machines that
-- already have the M1 shape applied. Safe because the table is empty.
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS tracking_processor_cursor

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS tracking_processor_cursor (
    company_id INT NOT NULL,
    vehicle_id INT NOT NULL,
    processor_kind VARCHAR(32) CHARACTER SET utf8mb4 COLLATE utf8mb4_unicode_ci NOT NULL,

    last_processed_ts TIMESTAMP NULL DEFAULT NULL,
    active_trip_id INT NULL DEFAULT NULL,

    probable_trip_started_at TIMESTAMP NULL DEFAULT NULL,
    probable_trip_start_lat DECIMAL(10,7) NULL DEFAULT NULL,
    probable_trip_start_lng DECIMAL(10,7) NULL DEFAULT NULL,
    probable_trip_start_position_id VARCHAR(64) CHARACTER SET utf8mb4 COLLATE utf8mb4_unicode_ci NULL DEFAULT NULL,
    probable_trip_start_centro_client_id INT NULL DEFAULT NULL,
    probable_trip_start_centro_center_id INT NULL DEFAULT NULL,
    probable_max_displacement_meters BIGINT NULL DEFAULT NULL,
    last_probable_position_id VARCHAR(64) CHARACTER SET utf8mb4 COLLATE utf8mb4_unicode_ci NULL DEFAULT NULL,

    tentative_centro_exit_count INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    tentative_centro_exit_first_ts TIMESTAMP NULL DEFAULT NULL,

    created_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    created_by VARCHAR(45) CHARACTER SET utf8mb4 COLLATE utf8mb4_unicode_ci NOT NULL,
    updated_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    updated_by VARCHAR(45) CHARACTER SET utf8mb4 COLLATE utf8mb4_unicode_ci NOT NULL,

    PRIMARY KEY (company_id, vehicle_id, processor_kind) USING BTREE,

    KEY idx_tpc_active_trip (company_id, active_trip_id) USING BTREE,
    KEY idx_tpc_probable_started (company_id, probable_trip_started_at) USING BTREE,
    KEY idx_tpc_created_by (company_id, created_by) USING BTREE,
    KEY idx_tpc_updated_by (company_id, updated_by) USING BTREE,

    CONSTRAINT fk_tpc_empresa
        FOREIGN KEY (company_id) REFERENCES empresa (company_id)
        ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE,
    CONSTRAINT fk_tpc_vehiculo
        FOREIGN KEY (company_id, vehicle_id) REFERENCES vehiculo (company_id, vehicle_id)
        ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE,
    -- active_trip_id is a soft pointer to a trip the cursor is currently
    -- driving. We do NOT declare a real FK for it: a composite FK on
    -- (company_id, active_trip_id) with ON DELETE SET NULL would try to
    -- null company_id too, which is non-nullable. ON DELETE RESTRICT would
    -- block trip deletion. Treating it as a soft pointer keeps the cursor
    -- table independent of trip-row lifecycle. The Phase 5 processor (and
    -- the 5-min sweep, M2a.7) must defensively re-load by PK and tolerate
    -- the "active_trip_id refers to a deleted trip" race — same way M1's
    -- ingest tolerates raced rows. No real FK declared here.
    CONSTRAINT fk_tpc_created_by
        FOREIGN KEY (company_id, created_by) REFERENCES empresa_usuario (company_id, user_name)
        ON DELETE RESTRICT ON UPDATE CASCADE,
    CONSTRAINT fk_tpc_updated_by
        FOREIGN KEY (company_id, updated_by) REFERENCES empresa_usuario (company_id, user_name)
        ON DELETE RESTRICT ON UPDATE CASCADE
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci ROW_FORMAT=DYNAMIC
archive/2026-05-12_001_trip_started_at_c:
-- Adds started_at_lat / started_at_lng to the `trip` table. Symmetric with
-- the existing ended_at_lat / ended_at_lng (introduced in
-- docs/migrations/already_run/2026-05-11_001_m2-trip-tables.sql).
--
-- Motivation: spec v0.2 §6.2 Albarán linker uses bilateral spatial
-- correlation (origin centro vs. trip start AND destination centro vs.
-- trip end). The end side already has stored coordinates; the start side
-- did not. Adding the symmetric columns avoids forcing the authoritative
-- linker to reach into Mongo at finalization time just to know "where
-- did this trip start, geographically".
--
-- Both columns are nullable: trips opened before this migration runs
-- will have NULL start coords until M2a.7 wires the PROBABLE→ACTIVE
-- promotion path to copy `cursor.probableTripStartLat/Lng` into them.
-- The Albarán linker treats null start coords as "no spatial signal"
-- and degrades to AUTO_LOW for that side — same fallback shape as the
-- "trip with no endedAt-coords" path already in OtLinker.

ALTER TABLE trip
    ADD COLUMN started_at_lat DECIMAL(10,7) NULL DEFAULT NULL AFTER started_at,
    ADD COLUMN started_at_lng DECIMAL(10,7) NULL DEFAULT NULL AFTER started_at_lat
archive/2026-05-12_002_nt-capacity-backf:
-- 2026-05-12_002_nt-capacity-backfill.sql
-- Backfill the `over_nt_capacity` badge columns for rows that ALREADY exist when
-- the NT capacity feature ships. Companion to 2026-05-12_001_nt-capacity-tracking.sql.
--
-- ── Why this migration exists ───────────────────────────────────────────────
-- The schema migration adds `over_nt_capacity TINYINT(1) NOT NULL DEFAULT 0` to
-- `mwm_retirada` and `albaran_ler`. The default is 0, so every pre-existing row
-- starts un-badged. Hooks flip the badge post-flush going forward, but they only
-- fire on writes — they do NOT walk the history.
--
-- For a compliance feature, shipping with 0-badged pre-existing over-capacity
-- rows means the day-1 dashboard lies. This migration computes the set of
-- (company_id, nt_id, codigo_ler, residue_id) keys whose cumulative consumed
-- mass exceeds the NT's authorized cantidad and flips the badge on every
-- contributing source row.
--
-- This migration does NOT raise `nt_capacity_alert` rows for historical
-- violations — alerts are an operational signal for *new* over-capacity events;
-- historical violations are surfaced exclusively through the badge + the
-- dashboard. If product later wants historical alert rows, that is a separate
-- backfill.
--
-- ── Idempotency ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
-- The UPDATE statements are idempotent: re-running them is a no-op for rows
-- whose badge is already correct, and the second pass would re-evaluate the
-- same set. Both statements use the same source-of-truth join (the @Computed
-- formula in NotificacionPreviaLer.consumedKg) so RETIRADA and ALBARAN_LER
-- badges agree.
--
-- ── Performance ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
-- The indexes added in the schema migration (`idx_mwm_retirada_nt_ler`,
-- `idx_albaran_ler_nt_ler`) make the GROUP BY in each derived table cheap.
-- For tenants with millions of LERs this still takes seconds; we use UPDATE
-- with a derived-table JOIN rather than a correlated subquery so MySQL can
-- materialize the over-capacity key set once.
--
-- Run during a maintenance window if either source table is very large (> 10M
-- rows). Operators can opt out by NOT applying this migration — the system
-- works correctly going forward without it, only the historical badges stay 0.

-- ── 1. Compute the over-capacity keys per tenant ───────────────────────────
-- A NotificacionPreviaLer is "over capacity" when:
--   SUM(MwmContenedor.neto where r.removal_id = c.removal_id and (nt_id, codigo_ler, residue_id) match)
--   + SUM(AlbaranLer.cantidad where (nt_id, codigo_ler, residue_id) match)
--   > NotificacionPreviaLer.cantidad
-- Mirrors the @Computed JPQL formula in
-- znapi/src/main/kotlin/com/zincapp/api/entity/contrato_tratamiento/NotificacionPreviaLer.kt:consumedKg.
--
-- Stored as a tmp table so both UPDATEs share the same key set without
-- re-computing the aggregation twice.
DROP TEMPORARY TABLE IF EXISTS _nt_capacity_backfill_over

CREATE TEMPORARY TABLE _nt_capacity_backfill_over (
    company_id  INT NOT NULL,
    nt_id       INT NOT NULL,
    codigo_ler  VARCHAR(10) NOT NULL,
    residue_id  INT NOT NULL,
    PRIMARY KEY (company_id, nt_id, codigo_ler, residue_id)
) ENGINE=InnoDB

INSERT INTO _nt_capacity_backfill_over (company_id, nt_id, codigo_ler, residue_id)
SELECT npl.company_id, npl.nt_id, npl.codigo_ler, npl.residue_id
FROM   notificacion_previa_ler npl
WHERE  npl.cantidad > 0
  AND  npl.cantidad < (
        COALESCE((
            SELECT SUM(c.neto)
            FROM   mwm_contenedor c
            JOIN   mwm_retirada   r
                   ON r.company_id = c.company_id
                   AND r.removal_id = c.removal_id
            WHERE  r.company_id = npl.company_id
              AND  r.nt_id      = npl.nt_id
              AND  r.codigo_ler = npl.codigo_ler
              AND  r.residue_id = npl.residue_id
        ), 0)
        +
        COALESCE((
            SELECT SUM(al.cantidad)
            FROM   albaran_ler al
            WHERE  al.company_id = npl.company_id
              AND  al.nt_id      = npl.nt_id
              AND  al.codigo_ler = npl.codigo_ler
              AND  al.residue_id = npl.residue_id
        ), 0)
      )

-- ── 2. Badge contributing mwm_retirada rows ─────────────────────────────────
UPDATE mwm_retirada r
JOIN   _nt_capacity_backfill_over o
       ON o.company_id = r.company_id
       AND o.nt_id      = r.nt_id
       AND o.codigo_ler = r.codigo_ler
       AND o.residue_id = r.residue_id
SET    r.over_nt_capacity = 1
WHERE  r.over_nt_capacity = 0

-- ── 3. Badge contributing albaran_ler rows ──────────────────────────────────
UPDATE albaran_ler al
JOIN   _nt_capacity_backfill_over o
       ON o.company_id = al.company_id
       AND o.nt_id      = al.nt_id
       AND o.codigo_ler = al.codigo_ler
       AND o.residue_id = al.residue_id
SET    al.over_nt_capacity = 1
WHERE  al.over_nt_capacity = 0
archive/2026-05-12_003_nt-capacity-track:
-- 2026-05-12_001_nt-capacity-tracking.sql
-- NT kg consumption tracking — schema additions for v1.
-- See docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-12-nt-kg-consumption-design.md.

-- 1. Badge columns on the two source tables. NOT NULL DEFAULT 0 = "no
-- validation has run on this row yet." Hooks flip these post-flush.
--
-- IMPORTANT — historical badges:
-- After this migration runs, every pre-existing row keeps over_nt_capacity=0.
-- The hooks only fire on writes — they do NOT walk the history. Run the
-- companion backfill `2026-05-12_002_nt-capacity-backfill.sql` immediately
-- after this migration to badge already-violating historical rows. Skipping
-- the backfill is supported (the feature works forward-only) but the
-- compliance dashboard will not surface historical violations.
ALTER TABLE mwm_retirada ADD COLUMN over_nt_capacity TINYINT(1) NOT NULL DEFAULT 0
ALTER TABLE albaran_ler  ADD COLUMN over_nt_capacity TINYINT(1) NOT NULL DEFAULT 0

-- 2. Alert audit table — append-only compliance history.
-- PK uses framework @AutoId convention (no AUTO_INCREMENT). See cuenta_ajuste.
CREATE TABLE nt_capacity_alert (
    company_id      INT          NOT NULL,
    alert_id        INT          NOT NULL,

    nt_id           INT          NOT NULL,
    codigo_ler      VARCHAR(10)  NOT NULL,
    residue_id      INT          NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,

    alert_type      ENUM('CAPACITY_EXCEEDED','UNAUTHORIZED_LER','NT_REDUCTION_EXCEEDED') NOT NULL,
    source_kind     ENUM('RETIRADA','ALBARAN','NT_REDUCTION') NOT NULL,
    retirada_id     INT          NULL,    -- populated when source_kind = RETIRADA;
                                          -- intentionally no FK so the audit row survives source deletion
    albaran_id      INT          NULL,    -- populated when source_kind = ALBARAN; same no-FK rationale
    albaran_residue_id INT       NULL,    -- the residue_id half of AlbaranLerPK
                                          -- (equals residue_id above for ALBARAN sources)

    authorized_kg   DECIMAL(12,3) NOT NULL,
    consumed_kg     DECIMAL(12,3) NOT NULL,
    overage_kg      DECIMAL(12,3) NOT NULL,

    status          ENUM('OPEN','ACKNOWLEDGED','RESOLVED','SUPERSEDED') NOT NULL DEFAULT 'OPEN',
    note            TEXT         NULL,
    superseded_by   INT          NULL,

    created_at      TIMESTAMP    NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    created_by      VARCHAR(45)  NOT NULL,
    updated_at      TIMESTAMP    NULL ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    updated_by      VARCHAR(45)  NULL,

    PRIMARY KEY (company_id, alert_id),
    KEY idx_nt_ler           (company_id, nt_id, codigo_ler, residue_id),
    KEY idx_open_by_created  (company_id, status, created_at),
    KEY idx_dedup_retirada   (company_id, retirada_id, nt_id, codigo_ler, residue_id, alert_type, status),
    KEY idx_dedup_albaran    (company_id, albaran_id, albaran_residue_id, nt_id, codigo_ler, residue_id, alert_type, status),
    KEY idx_dedup_nt         (company_id, nt_id, codigo_ler, residue_id, alert_type, status)
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci ROW_FORMAT=DYNAMIC

-- 3. Read-path indexes for the @Computed JPQL subqueries.
-- Probe before adding — drop adds that an existing index already covers.
-- SHOW INDEX FROM mwm_retirada\G
-- SHOW INDEX FROM albaran_ler\G
CREATE INDEX idx_mwm_retirada_nt_ler ON mwm_retirada (company_id, nt_id, codigo_ler, residue_id)
CREATE INDEX idx_albaran_ler_nt_ler  ON albaran_ler  (company_id, nt_id, codigo_ler, residue_id)

-- 4. Register the new ACL module. _module_notification.module has an FK on
-- _module(module); we ship no notifications in v1 but future additions need
-- the parent row, and the permission admin UI surfaces modules from here.
INSERT IGNORE INTO _module (`module`, actions)
VALUES ('compliance', '["get","list","modify","query","web-access"]')
archive/2026-05-14_003_empresa-data-aeat:
-- =============================================================================
-- AEAT certificate Vault migration columns.
--
-- Adds Vault-encrypted certificate storage alongside the legacy V1 `binary_data`
-- column. The legacy column is preserved during the 30-day rollback window and
-- nulled later by a cleanup task (see plan #4, post-rollout cleanup).
--
-- Columns:
--   aeat_cert_encrypted          MEDIUMBLOB   — opaque Vault blob for the p12 bytes
--   aeat_cert_password_encrypted MEDIUMBLOB   — opaque Vault blob for the p12 PIN
--   aeat_cert_alias              VARCHAR(255) — plaintext metadata (keystore alias),
--                                              NOT part of AAD
--   aeat_cert_migration_state    VARCHAR(16)  — NONE | LEGACY_V1 | MIGRATED
--   aeat_cert_migrated_at        DATETIME     — set when transitioning to MIGRATED;
--                                              drives the 30-day cleanup window
--
-- Initial state derivation:
--   binary_data IS NULL      → NONE
--   binary_data IS NOT NULL  → LEGACY_V1
--
-- See docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-13-vault-kms-integration-design.md §7.2.
-- =============================================================================

ALTER TABLE empresa_data
    ADD COLUMN aeat_cert_encrypted MEDIUMBLOB NULL AFTER binary_data,
    ADD COLUMN aeat_cert_password_encrypted MEDIUMBLOB NULL AFTER aeat_cert_encrypted,
    ADD COLUMN aeat_cert_alias VARCHAR(255) NULL AFTER aeat_cert_password_encrypted,
    ADD COLUMN aeat_cert_migration_state VARCHAR(16) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'NONE'
        AFTER aeat_cert_alias,
    ADD COLUMN aeat_cert_migrated_at DATETIME NULL AFTER aeat_cert_migration_state

-- Derive state for existing rows.
UPDATE empresa_data
    SET aeat_cert_migration_state = 'LEGACY_V1'
  WHERE binary_data IS NOT NULL
    AND aeat_cert_migration_state = 'NONE'

-- Invariant CHECK constraint:
--   NONE      → all encrypted cols NULL, binary_data NULL.
--   LEGACY_V1 → binary_data NOT NULL, encrypted cols NULL.
--   MIGRATED  → encrypted cols NOT NULL, alias NOT NULL, aeat_cert_migrated_at NOT NULL.
--               binary_data MAY still be present during the 30-day rollback window.
ALTER TABLE empresa_data
    ADD CONSTRAINT empresa_data_aeat_state_invariant CHECK (
        (aeat_cert_migration_state = 'NONE'
            AND binary_data IS NULL
            AND aeat_cert_encrypted IS NULL
            AND aeat_cert_password_encrypted IS NULL
            AND aeat_cert_alias IS NULL
            AND aeat_cert_migrated_at IS NULL)
        OR
        (aeat_cert_migration_state = 'LEGACY_V1'
            AND binary_data IS NOT NULL
            AND aeat_cert_encrypted IS NULL
            AND aeat_cert_password_encrypted IS NULL
            AND aeat_cert_alias IS NULL
            AND aeat_cert_migrated_at IS NULL)
        OR
        (aeat_cert_migration_state = 'MIGRATED'
            AND aeat_cert_encrypted IS NOT NULL
            AND aeat_cert_password_encrypted IS NOT NULL
            AND aeat_cert_alias IS NOT NULL
            AND aeat_cert_migrated_at IS NOT NULL)
    )
archive/2026-05-14_004_empresa-data-bina:
-- =============================================================================
-- Relax empresa_data.binary_data to NULLABLE.
--
-- The 2026-05-14_003 CHECK constraint requires `binary_data IS NULL` when
-- aeat_cert_migration_state='NONE' (the default state for newly-inserted rows).
-- But the column was NOT NULL on the original schema, making INSERTs of
-- no-cert rows impossible (would violate NOT NULL even when CHECK is met).
--
-- Same pattern as 2026-05-14_002 for empresa_config.value. Plaintext (legacy)
-- rows still allowed: NULL is permitted, not required.
--
-- Column type: TEXT (NOT LONGBLOB). The original schema declares the column
-- as `TEXT CHARACTER SET utf8mb4` and `BinaryDataConverter` is a JSON
-- AttributeConverter<T?, String?>; EclipseLink reads/writes it as a String.
-- Touching the column type to LONGBLOB would silently break the JPA read
-- path with a ClassCastException ([B → String) once any legacy row is
-- actually queried (see TransitionalCertificateAccess / migrate flow). The
-- column was briefly changed in an earlier draft and reverted here.
--
-- See docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-13-vault-kms-integration-design.md §7.2.
-- =============================================================================

ALTER TABLE empresa_data MODIFY COLUMN binary_data TEXT NULL
archive/2026-05-14_005_crypto-module-row:
-- =============================================================================
-- Seed `_module` with the `crypto` module row.
--
-- Required so ops can set the Plan #3/#4 rollout flags in `empresa_config`:
--   (module='crypto', setting='aeat_migration.enabled')
--   (module='crypto', setting='sensitive_config_backfill.enabled')
--
-- Without this row, `empresa_config.module` FK to `_module(module)` rejects any
-- attempt to insert a flag — so TransitionalCertificateAccess.getOrMigrate
-- always reads `flagEnabled = false` (silent migration never triggers) and
-- SensitiveConfigBackfillTask short-circuits on every tenant ("flag disabled").
-- The entire rollout mechanism is non-functional until this row exists.
--
-- INSERT IGNORE so re-running the migration is a no-op.
--
-- See docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-13-vault-kms-integration-design.md §8.2, §10.1.
-- =============================================================================

INSERT IGNORE INTO _module (`module`) VALUES ('crypto')
archive/2026-05-18_001_import-plan-stagi:
-- 2026-05-18_001_import-plan-staging.sql
-- Cluster-safe staging for Lapaz import plans + uploaded bytes.
--
-- The legacy in-process ImportPlanCache (Caffeine) only lived on the analyzing
-- node, so HAProxy round-robin could route the apply request to a different
-- Payara node and miss the plan ("AnalysisPlan <id> not found"). These two
-- tables move plan + raw bytes to MySQL so every node sees them.
--
-- TTL is enforced opportunistically by ImportPlanCache.put()/get() — every
-- write deletes rows past expires_at. The DAILY ImportPlanCleanupTask is the
-- backstop for dormant tenants (those who run an import once and never come
-- back) so abandoned plan + bytes don't accumulate.
--
-- `created_by` carries the originating user so the front-end can surface a
-- "you have a pending import" banner scoped to *that* user, plus enforce a
-- one-pending-plan-per-user rule on analyze (over-consumption guard).

CREATE TABLE _import_plan (
    plan_id     VARCHAR(36) NOT NULL,
    company_id  INT         NOT NULL,
    created_by  VARCHAR(45) NOT NULL,
    plan_json   LONGTEXT    NOT NULL,
    file_count  INT         NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    item_count  INT         NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    expires_at  TIMESTAMP   NOT NULL,
    created_at  TIMESTAMP   NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    PRIMARY KEY (plan_id),
    KEY idx_expires_at      (expires_at),
    KEY idx_company_user    (company_id, created_by, expires_at),
    CONSTRAINT fk_import_plan_user
        FOREIGN KEY (company_id, created_by)
        REFERENCES empresa_usuario (company_id, user_name)
        ON UPDATE CASCADE ON DELETE CASCADE
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci ROW_FORMAT=DYNAMIC

-- Bytes split out so plan_json reads don't drag LONGBLOBs into the buffer
-- pool. ON DELETE CASCADE keeps the two tables in lockstep when remove() or
-- the TTL sweep evicts a plan.
CREATE TABLE _import_plan_bytes (
    plan_id VARCHAR(36) NOT NULL,
    sha256  VARCHAR(64) NOT NULL,
    bytes   LONGBLOB    NOT NULL,
    PRIMARY KEY (plan_id, sha256),
    CONSTRAINT fk_import_plan_bytes_plan
        FOREIGN KEY (plan_id) REFERENCES _import_plan (plan_id) ON DELETE CASCADE
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci ROW_FORMAT=DYNAMIC
archive/2026-05-18_002_mwm-retirada-kg-d:
-- 2026-05-18_002_mwm-retirada-kg-di.sql
-- Declared weight on the DI document for each retirada.
--
-- `kg_di` is the *legal* truth — the kg figure declared on the MITECO DI
-- (Documento de Identificación de Traslado de Residuos). It is distinct from
-- the operational sum-of-containers exposed as the @Computed `kgNeto` field
-- on MwmRetirada:
--
--   • `kgNeto` (computed)  = SUM(MwmContenedor.neto)            — what we weighed
--   • `kg_di`  (this col)  = declared kg on the DI PDF / form    — what we filed
--
-- When the two diverge the outputs table flags the row so the operator can
-- investigate (label mis-counts, partial weighings, etc.).
--
-- Populated three ways:
--   1. Lapaz importer extracts the kg from the DI PDF (best-effort regex).
--   2. The DCS-edit popover lets the operator type/correct the value.
--   3. Future bulk-import flows can backfill from MITECO exports.
--
-- Nullable on purpose — many existing retiradas predate this column and we
-- don't want to forge a number we don't have.

ALTER TABLE mwm_retirada
    ADD COLUMN kg_di DECIMAL(12,3) NULL AFTER contenedores
archive/2026-05-19_001_empresa-credencia:
-- =============================================================================
-- Plan #6 Task 1 — empresa_credencial + empresa_credencial_audit
--
-- Replaces the AEAT-cert-specific `empresa_data` + the
-- `SensitiveConfigCatalog`-driven empresa_config encryption with a single
-- dedicated credential store. Migration of existing data (3 LEGACY_V1 AEAT
-- certs, 11 eSIR pairs, 10 mail-config blobs) lands in Plan #6 Task 8.
--
-- Schema rationale: see
-- docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-19-empresa-credencial-design.md §3.1.
-- =============================================================================

CREATE TABLE empresa_credencial (
  company_id            INT          NOT NULL,
  credencial_id         INT          NOT NULL,                 -- @AutoId (no AUTO_INCREMENT; app-managed via composite-PK @AutoId)
  tipo                  VARCHAR(50)  NOT NULL,                 -- TipoCredencial enum literal: 'AEAT' | 'ESIR' | 'MAIL' | 'WEBHOOK'
  nombre                VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,                 -- 'default' (most), 'payrolls' for hr.payrolls mail-config
  user_name             VARCHAR(45)  DEFAULT NULL,             -- NULL = tenant-scoped, populated = per-user override (MAIL only)
  username              VARCHAR(255) DEFAULT NULL,             -- plaintext credential login (SMTP login, eSIR username, AEAT alias)
  secrets_encrypted     LONGBLOB     DEFAULT NULL,             -- Vault-encrypted JSON; shape per type (see spec §3.3)
  cn                    VARCHAR(255) DEFAULT NULL,             -- AEAT subject CN (first-class for UI display)
  expires_at            DATE         DEFAULT NULL,             -- AEAT cert notAfter (first-class for UI display)
  metadata_json         JSON         DEFAULT NULL,             -- non-sensitive type-specific extras (endpoint URL, from address, etc.)
  is_active             BOOLEAN      NOT NULL DEFAULT TRUE,
  retired_at            DATETIME     DEFAULT NULL,             -- soft-delete: row hidden from resolution
  last_used_at          DATETIME     DEFAULT NULL,             -- cheap heartbeat — no audit row per use
  created_by            VARCHAR(45)  NOT NULL,
  created_at            DATETIME     NOT NULL,
  updated_by            VARCHAR(45)  NOT NULL,
  updated_at            DATETIME     NOT NULL,
  -- Generated column enforces "at most one active row per (company, tipo, nombre, user_name)".
  -- COALESCE so tenant-scoped (user_name NULL) and per-user rows can coexist in the same slot.
  active_slot_key       VARCHAR(205) GENERATED ALWAYS AS (
                          CASE WHEN is_active = TRUE AND retired_at IS NULL
                               THEN CONCAT_WS('|', tipo, nombre, COALESCE(user_name, ''))
                          END
                        ) STORED,
  PRIMARY KEY (company_id, credencial_id),
  UNIQUE KEY uk_active_slot (company_id, active_slot_key),
  KEY idx_user (company_id, user_name),
  KEY idx_last_used (company_id, last_used_at),
  CONSTRAINT chk_credencial_user_name CHECK (user_name IS NULL OR LENGTH(user_name) > 0),
  CONSTRAINT fk_credencial_empresa
    FOREIGN KEY (company_id) REFERENCES empresa(company_id)
    ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE,
  CONSTRAINT fk_credencial_created_by
    FOREIGN KEY (company_id, created_by) REFERENCES empresa_usuario(company_id, user_name)
    ON DELETE RESTRICT ON UPDATE CASCADE,
  CONSTRAINT fk_credencial_updated_by
    FOREIGN KEY (company_id, updated_by) REFERENCES empresa_usuario(company_id, user_name)
    ON DELETE RESTRICT ON UPDATE CASCADE
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci

CREATE TABLE empresa_credencial_audit (
  company_id            INT          NOT NULL,
  audit_id              INT          NOT NULL,                 -- @AutoId
  -- Denormalized credential identity (no FK — audit must survive credential deletion).
  -- credencial_id is NULLABLE: CREATE/FAILED rows fire when the credential never
  -- persisted (e.g. V1 decrypt threw before insert).
  credencial_id         INT          DEFAULT NULL,
  tipo                  VARCHAR(50)  NOT NULL,
  nombre                VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
  action                ENUM('CREATE','UPDATE','DELETE','REVEAL','VALIDATE') NOT NULL,
  user_name             VARCHAR(45)  NOT NULL,                 -- actor (session user), not the credential's user
  user_ip               VARCHAR(45)  DEFAULT NULL,
  outcome               VARCHAR(20)  NOT NULL,                 -- 'SUCCESS' | 'FAILED'
  message               VARCHAR(500) DEFAULT NULL,             -- short, redacted, free-text (never plaintext secrets)
  created_at            DATETIME     NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
  PRIMARY KEY (company_id, audit_id),
  KEY idx_credencial (company_id, credencial_id, created_at),
  KEY idx_action (company_id, action, created_at),
  KEY idx_user (company_id, user_name, created_at),
  -- Backs the /status `failed` count query: per (tenant, tipo, nombre, user_name),
  -- find the most-recent audit row, filter by action=CREATE outcome=FAILED.
  KEY idx_audit_status (company_id, tipo, nombre, user_name, action, outcome, created_at),
  CONSTRAINT fk_credencial_audit_empresa
    FOREIGN KEY (company_id) REFERENCES empresa(company_id)
    ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci
-- 2026-05-19_001_mwm-retirada-log.sql
-- Per-row audit log for mwm_retirada, wired to the framework's automatic
-- audit-on-update mechanism (com.zincapp.ws.entity.AuditableEntity).
--
-- When MwmRetirada is mutated through the framework merge path
-- (`endpoint.updateResourceAllowing(...)`), `audit(obj)` in
-- ws/.../endpoint/extensions/security/Audit.kt detects the
-- `AuditableEntity<*, *>` marker, computes the diff between old and new
-- entity state, and persists a child row here with:
--
--   audit_change   structured AuditChange (updates / insertions / deletions)
--   user_name      session userName  (FK to empresa_usuario)
--   user_role      session role label
--   user_ip        request source ip ('?' when unavailable)
--   audit_ts       moment of the change
--
-- `created_at` / `created_by` are kept for parity with the GastoLog
-- precedent (ZnNonUpdatableEntity gives them for free); in practice they
-- duplicate audit_ts/user_name but cost nothing.
--
-- ON DELETE CASCADE — when a retirada is deleted, its history goes too.

CREATE TABLE mwm_retirada_log (
    company_id    INT          NOT NULL,
    removal_id    INT          NOT NULL,
    log_id        INT          NOT NULL,
    audit_change  JSON         DEFAULT NULL,
    user_name     VARCHAR(45)  CHARACTER SET utf8mb4 COLLATE utf8mb4_unicode_ci NOT NULL,
    user_role     VARCHAR(45)  CHARACTER SET utf8mb4 COLLATE utf8mb4_unicode_ci NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
    user_ip       VARCHAR(45)  CHARACTER SET utf8mb4 COLLATE utf8mb4_unicode_ci NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
    audit_ts      DATETIME(6)  NOT NULL,
    created_at    TIMESTAMP    NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    created_by    VARCHAR(45)  CHARACTER SET utf8mb4 COLLATE utf8mb4_unicode_ci NOT NULL,
    PRIMARY KEY (company_id, removal_id, log_id) USING BTREE,
    KEY fk_mwm_retirada_log_user_name (company_id, user_name) USING BTREE,
    KEY fk_mwm_retirada_log_created_by (company_id, created_by) USING BTREE,
    KEY ix_mwm_retirada_log_audit_ts (audit_ts) USING BTREE,
    CONSTRAINT fk_mwm_retirada_log_user_name
        FOREIGN KEY (company_id, user_name)
        REFERENCES empresa_usuario (company_id, user_name)
        ON DELETE RESTRICT ON UPDATE CASCADE,
    CONSTRAINT fk_mwm_retirada_log_created_by
        FOREIGN KEY (company_id, created_by)
        REFERENCES empresa_usuario (company_id, user_name)
        ON DELETE RESTRICT ON UPDATE CASCADE,
    CONSTRAINT fk_mwm_retirada_log_retirada
        FOREIGN KEY (company_id, removal_id)
        REFERENCES mwm_retirada (company_id, removal_id)
        ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci ROW_FORMAT=DYNAMIC
archive/2026-05-20_001_seguro-rename-ins:
-- =============================================================================
-- 2026-05-20_001_seguro-rename-insurance-client-id.sql
--
-- Renames seguro.insurance_company_id -> insurance_client_id to match the
-- Seguro entity mapping: the field is now `insuranceClientId` and the
-- `aseguradora` ManyToOne joins entidad.client_id via this column.
--
-- Also drops the now-orphan fk_seguro_aseguradora FK (-> _aseguradora) and its
-- backing single-column index: no entity field references _aseguradora anymore.
-- Dropping a constraint can never fail on existing data, and every row already
-- satisfies fk_seguro_entidad, so this is data-safe.
--
-- IDEMPOTENT: guarded on the presence of the old column. The e2e baseline
-- (e2e/mysql/init.d/02-schema.sql) already carries the final state, so when
-- this file is auto-applied as init.d/04-pending-migrations.sql every step is a
-- no-op there. On production (old column present) the full rename runs.
--
-- DEPLOY ORDER: run this BEFORE deploying the WAR that maps insurance_client_id,
-- otherwise EclipseLink fails to map the `seguro` entity at startup.
-- =============================================================================

SET @old_col := (
    SELECT COUNT(*) FROM information_schema.COLUMNS
    WHERE TABLE_SCHEMA = DATABASE()
      AND TABLE_NAME = 'seguro'
      AND COLUMN_NAME = 'insurance_company_id'
)

-- 1. Drop both FKs sitting on the old column.
SET @sql := IF(@old_col = 1,
    'ALTER TABLE seguro DROP FOREIGN KEY fk_seguro_aseguradora, DROP FOREIGN KEY fk_seguro_entidad',
    'SELECT 1')

-- 2. Drop the orphan single-column index that backed fk_seguro_aseguradora.
SET @sql := IF(@old_col = 1,
    'ALTER TABLE seguro DROP KEY fk_seguro_aseguradora',
    'SELECT 1')

-- 3. Rename the column. MySQL auto-updates the composite index
--    fk_seguro_entidad (company_id, <col>) to the new column name.
SET @sql := IF(@old_col = 1,
    'ALTER TABLE seguro CHANGE COLUMN insurance_company_id insurance_client_id INT DEFAULT NULL',
    'SELECT 1')

-- 4. Re-add the entidad FK on the renamed column (reuses the composite index).
SET @sql := IF(@old_col = 1,
    'ALTER TABLE seguro ADD CONSTRAINT fk_seguro_entidad FOREIGN KEY (company_id, insurance_client_id) REFERENCES entidad (company_id, client_id) ON DELETE RESTRICT ON UPDATE CASCADE',
    'SELECT 1')
archive/2026-05-27_001_credencial-tipo-o:
-- =============================================================================
-- Gate 2.10 — defense-in-depth CHECK constraints on empresa_credencial &
-- empresa_credencial_audit identity fields.
--
-- The application layer enforces:
--   - TipoCredencial enum membership at parse time (CredencialValidatorDispatch)
--   - identity-field immutability on PUT (CredencialService.update)
-- These DB-level constraints catch any path that bypasses the app layer (raw
-- SQL fixtures, manual operator edits, future bugs in the validator dispatch).
--
-- `outcome` is unconstrained VARCHAR(20) today — a future typo in the audit
-- emitter could silently break the /status `failed` rollup query without any
-- audible signal. Pinning the closed set in SQL surfaces it as a constraint
-- violation at write time.
-- =============================================================================

ALTER TABLE empresa_credencial
  ADD CONSTRAINT chk_credencial_tipo
    CHECK (tipo IN ('AEAT', 'ESIR', 'MAIL', 'WEBHOOK'))

ALTER TABLE empresa_credencial_audit
  ADD CONSTRAINT chk_credencial_audit_tipo
    CHECK (tipo IN ('AEAT', 'ESIR', 'MAIL', 'WEBHOOK')),
  ADD CONSTRAINT chk_credencial_audit_outcome
    CHECK (outcome IN ('SUCCESS', 'FAILED', 'SKIPPED'))
archive/2026-05-30_001_operator-grant.sq:
-- Platform-operator grant: which developer accounts are operator-CAPABLE.
-- Global (non-tenant) table, mirroring _developer_account. Eligibility to operate
-- across tenants is `partner.internal = TRUE` (ZincApp); CAPABILITY is an active row
-- here. Neither grants live tenant access on its own — that requires an audited
-- elevation (Phase 2). A grant is global (the target tenant is chosen per-elevation),
-- per the locked design decision.

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS _developer_operator_grant (
    grant_id      INT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
    account_id    INT NOT NULL,
    -- Optional capability scope label (e.g. 'support', 'billing'); NULL = full operator.
    -- Reserved for future per-category narrowing; not enforced in Phase 1.
    scope         VARCHAR(100) NULL,
    active        BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT TRUE,
    granted_by    VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,           -- operator/admin username that created the grant
    granted_at    TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    revoked_by    VARCHAR(255) NULL,
    revoked_at    TIMESTAMP NULL,
    created_at    TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    updated_at    TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    -- One active grant per (account, scope); revoked rows kept for audit.
    UNIQUE KEY uk_operator_grant_account_scope (account_id, scope),
    CONSTRAINT fk_operator_grant_account FOREIGN KEY (account_id) REFERENCES _developer_account (account_id) ON DELETE CASCADE
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4
archive/2026-05-30_002_operator-action.s:
-- Platform-operator action log: append-only audit of cross-tenant operator activity.
-- GLOBAL (non-tenant) by nature — operators act across tenants — so this is a plain
-- auto-increment table (NOT a CompanyPK tenant table). No FK to empresa: the audit must
-- survive tenant deletion and is self-describing (target_company_id denormalized).
--
-- Lifecycle: an OPEN row is committed at elevation start (BEFORE the elevated session is
-- synthesized — Codex #3 fail-closed); it is CLOSED on logout/timeout (Phase 4). A
-- request_id (server UUIDv7) correlates business writes to the operator who made them.

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS _operator_action (
    action_id          BIGINT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
    -- The elevation this row belongs to (UUIDv7); shared by the OPEN row and per-request rows.
    intent_id          VARCHAR(64) NOT NULL,
    -- Server-generated correlation id of the request that produced this row (UUIDv7).
    request_id         VARCHAR(64) NULL,
    -- The real human behind the operator session (developer username / _sysadmin today).
    operator           VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    -- Tenant the operator elevated into. 0/NULL only for non-tenant-scoped operator actions.
    target_company_id  INT NOT NULL,
    -- ELEVATE_OPEN | ELEVATE_CLOSE | ELEVATE_FAILED | ACTION (a write performed while elevated).
    action_type        VARCHAR(30) NOT NULL,
    -- success | failed | open | closed
    outcome            VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL,
    -- Operator-supplied justification (added at session close, Phase 4); NULL until then.
    reason             VARCHAR(1000) NULL,
    -- Optional structured detail (endpoint path, error, etc.).
    message            VARCHAR(1000) NULL,
    operator_ip        VARCHAR(45) NULL,
    created_at         TIMESTAMP(3) NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP(3),
    closed_at          TIMESTAMP(3) NULL,
    KEY idx_operator_action_intent (intent_id),
    KEY idx_operator_action_request (request_id),
    KEY idx_operator_action_operator (operator, created_at),
    KEY idx_operator_action_tenant (target_company_id, created_at)
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4
archive/2026-06-03_001_operator-grant-sc:
-- Operator-grant scope integrity: a non-null scope must be one of the 6 known operator permissions.
-- NULL stays allowed (= full operator / ALL). Defends against direct-DB bogus-scope inserts that
-- the API layer already rejects (DeveloperOperatorGrantEndpoint scope validation).
-- MySQL 8.0.16+ enforces CHECK constraints (the project targets MySQL 8).
--
-- Idempotent: MySQL has no `ADD CONSTRAINT IF NOT EXISTS`, and a bare ADD errors on re-run, so the
-- constraint is added only if it is absent (checked via information_schema). Safe to re-run after a
-- partial migration apply. Guard scoped to the current schema (DATABASE()).

DROP PROCEDURE IF EXISTS _add_operator_grant_scope_check

CREATE PROCEDURE _add_operator_grant_scope_check()
BEGIN
    IF NOT EXISTS (
        SELECT 1 FROM information_schema.TABLE_CONSTRAINTS
        WHERE CONSTRAINT_SCHEMA = DATABASE()
          AND TABLE_NAME = '_developer_operator_grant'
          AND CONSTRAINT_NAME = 'chk_operator_grant_scope'
    ) THEN
        ALTER TABLE _developer_operator_grant
          ADD CONSTRAINT chk_operator_grant_scope
          CHECK (scope IS NULL OR scope IN ('sysadmin','printables','migration','scheduler','tracking','grants'));
    END IF;
END
archive/2026-06-03_002_platform-action.s:
-- Tenantless platform-admin action log: append-only audit of platform-wide admin activity that is
-- NOT bound to a tenant or an elevation intent (sysadmin / printables / scheduler / grants surfaces
-- reached via the isPlatformAdmin capability, without elevating into a tenant). Sibling of
-- _operator_action; kept separate so _operator_action.target_company_id stays non-null and the
-- elevation audit queries never special-case null.

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS _platform_action (
    action_id   BIGINT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
    operator    VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,   -- the real human (developer username / _sysadmin)
    request_id  VARCHAR(64)  NULL,       -- server UUIDv7; correlates to business writes
    method      VARCHAR(10)  NOT NULL,   -- POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE
    path        VARCHAR(512) NOT NULL,   -- the admin endpoint path
    status      INT          NOT NULL,   -- HTTP status
    operator_ip VARCHAR(45)  NULL,
    created_at  TIMESTAMP(3) NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP(3),
    KEY idx_platform_action_operator (operator, created_at),
    KEY idx_platform_action_request (request_id)
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4
archive/2026-06-04_001_operator-grant-co:
-- Per-tenant elevation scoping for operator grants.
--
-- Until now operator grants are GLOBAL: any grant holder can elevate into ANY company. This adds an
-- OPTIONAL allow-list of companies per grant. Semantics (backward-compatible):
--   * A grant with NO rows here  → unrestricted (today's behavior — may elevate into any tenant).
--   * A grant with >=1 row here   → may ONLY elevate into the listed companies.
-- The union is taken across an operator's active grants (ElevationService.allowedElevationCompanies).
--
-- Join table (not a column on _developer_operator_grant) so the (account_id, scope) grant model and
-- its UNIQUE key stay intact, and "all companies" is the natural empty-set default.
--
-- Idempotent: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS. The FK cascades on grant delete; company_id is a bare int
-- (no FK to empresa — a grant's allow-list must survive tenant lifecycle the same way _operator_action
-- denormalizes target_company_id and keeps no empresa FK).

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS _developer_operator_grant_company (
    grant_id    INT NOT NULL,
    company_id  INT NOT NULL,
    PRIMARY KEY (grant_id, company_id),
    KEY idx_grant_company_company (company_id),
    CONSTRAINT fk_grant_company_grant FOREIGN KEY (grant_id)
        REFERENCES _developer_operator_grant (grant_id) ON DELETE CASCADE
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4
archive/2026-06-04_002_pending-device.sq:
-- 2026-06-04_002_pending-device.sql
-- Tenantless pool of unknown IMEIs seen at the gateway notify gate, awaiting
-- provisioning into tracking_device. No company_id: an unknown device has no
-- tenant until an operator provisions it. PK = imei (globally unique, matching
-- tracking_device.uq_tracking_device_imei).
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS  pending_device (
    imei          VARCHAR(20)  NOT NULL,
    first_seen    DATETIME(6)  NOT NULL,
    last_seen     DATETIME(6)  NOT NULL,
    attempts      INT          NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
    model         VARCHAR(32)  NULL,

    PRIMARY KEY (imei),
    KEY idx_pending_device_last_seen (last_seen)
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci
archive/2026-06-07_001_albaran-sealed-lo:
-- Manual seal/unseal lock for albaran. Additive only — does NOT touch the
-- `vinculable` VIRTUAL generated column (automation now skips a locked `sealed`,
-- so `sealed` already holds the effective value).
ALTER TABLE albaran
  ADD COLUMN sealed_locked_by VARCHAR(45) CHARACTER SET utf8mb4 COLLATE utf8mb4_unicode_ci NULL AFTER sealed,
  ADD COLUMN sealed_locked_at DATETIME NULL AFTER sealed_locked_by,
  ADD CONSTRAINT fk_albaran_sealed_locked_by
      FOREIGN KEY (company_id, sealed_locked_by)
      REFERENCES empresa_usuario (company_id, user_name)
      ON DELETE RESTRICT ON UPDATE CASCADE,
  ADD INDEX idx_albaran_sealed_lock (company_id, sealed_locked_at)
archive/2026-06-08_001_developer-account:
-- WebAuthn passkey credentials for DEVELOPER accounts (internal-partner devs / operators).
--
-- Sibling of `empresa_usuario_passkey` (tenant users) — same 12 WebAuthn-neutral columns, but a GLOBAL
-- single-owner identity (account_id) instead of the tenant composite key. The shared WebAuthn ceremony
-- (WebAuthnCeremonyService) is principal-agnostic; only this storage row differs. See
-- docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-08-developer-passkeys-iam-design.md.
--
-- This is the enrollment surface that unblocks flipping `internalUserPasswordLocked = true` in prod
-- (internal devs must enroll a passkey or SSO before password login is sealed).
--
-- credential_id MUST be globally unique across BOTH passkey tables — discoverable login resolves the
-- principal FROM the credential by federating the lookup across tenant + developer stores.
--
-- Idempotent: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS.

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS _developer_account_passkey (
    account_id       INT             NOT NULL,
    passkey_id       INT             NOT NULL,
    credential_id    VARBINARY(1024) NOT NULL,
    public_key_cbor  BLOB            NOT NULL,
    aaguid           BINARY(16)      NULL,
    sign_count       BIGINT          NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    display_name     VARCHAR(100)    NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
    transports       VARCHAR(200)    NULL,
    discoverable     TINYINT(1)      NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
    backed_up        TINYINT(1)      NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    attestation_fmt  VARCHAR(50)     NULL,
    last_used_at     DATETIME(3)     NULL,
    last_used_ip     VARCHAR(45)     NULL,
    created_at       DATETIME(3)     NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP(3),
    PRIMARY KEY (account_id, passkey_id),
    UNIQUE INDEX idx_dev_passkey_credential_id (credential_id(255)),
    CONSTRAINT fk_dev_passkey_account
        FOREIGN KEY (account_id)
        REFERENCES _developer_account (account_id)
        ON DELETE CASCADE
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci
archive/2026-06-08_002_api-credential.sq:
-- Developer-portal API keys (global, partner-scoped credentials).
--
-- Backs the IAM portal's "API Keys" / Credentials surface (FE: credentials.ts + CredentialsPage.tsx,
-- calling /private/partner/credentials). That FE previously pointed at a phantom `ApiCredential` entity
-- that never existed on the backend — a non-functional stub. This is the real storage row.
--
-- Sibling of `_developer_agent_token` (same global IDENTITY PK + hash-on-store shape), but label-only:
-- an API key grants access by partner, not by capability, so there is NO scopes column. Only the SHA-256
-- hex hash of the key is stored (`key_hash`); the full key is shown exactly once on creation.
--
-- credential_id is a SINGLE-COLUMN `GenerationType.IDENTITY` PK, which in MySQL REQUIRES AUTO_INCREMENT
-- (the "never AUTO_INCREMENT" project rule applies only to composite @AutoId PKs, not single-col globals —
-- mirrors `_developer_agent_token`).
--
-- Idempotent: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS.

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS _api_credential (
    credential_id  INT          NOT NULL AUTO_INCREMENT,
    partner_id     INT          NOT NULL,
    account_id     INT          NOT NULL,
    label          VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    key_hash       VARCHAR(64)  NOT NULL,
    key_prefix     VARCHAR(20)  NOT NULL,
    active         TINYINT(1)   NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
    expires_at     DATETIME(3)  NULL,
    last_used_at   DATETIME(3)  NULL,
    last_used_ip   VARCHAR(45)  NULL,
    created_at     DATETIME(3)  NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP(3),
    updated_at     DATETIME(3)  NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP(3),
    PRIMARY KEY (credential_id),
    UNIQUE INDEX idx_api_credential_key_hash (key_hash),
    CONSTRAINT fk_api_credential_account
        FOREIGN KEY (account_id) REFERENCES _developer_account (account_id) ON DELETE CASCADE
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci
-- WasteSubmission: system-of-record for eSIR DCS submissions.
-- No AUTO_INCREMENT (composite PK; IDs are app-managed via @AutoId).
-- Do NOT pre-seed _internal_counter: the @AutoId generator lazy-inits a missing
-- counter from SELECT COALESCE(MAX(id),0)+1, which correctly continues after the
-- backfill below.

CREATE TABLE waste_submission (
  company_id INT NOT NULL,
  submission_id INT NOT NULL,
  platform VARCHAR(16) NOT NULL,
  document_type VARCHAR(8) NOT NULL,
  document_number VARCHAR(40) NOT NULL,
  status VARCHAR(16) NOT NULL,
  phase VARCHAR(2) NULL,
  csv VARCHAR(64) NULL,
  submitted_at TIMESTAMP NULL,
  platform_result_code VARCHAR(32) NULL,
  attempts INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  next_attempt_at TIMESTAMP NULL,
  albaran_id INT NULL,
  codigo_ler VARCHAR(20) NULL,
  residue_id INT NULL,
  nt_id INT NULL,
  created_by VARCHAR(45) NULL,
  created_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
  updated_by VARCHAR(45) NULL,
  updated_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
  PRIMARY KEY (company_id, submission_id),
  UNIQUE KEY uq_waste_submission_slot (company_id, platform, document_type, document_number),
  KEY idx_ws_albaran (company_id, albaran_id, codigo_ler, residue_id)
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4

CREATE TABLE waste_submission_event (
  company_id INT NOT NULL,
  event_id INT NOT NULL,
  submission_id INT NOT NULL,
  phase VARCHAR(2) NULL,
  csv VARCHAR(64) NULL,
  platform_result_code VARCHAR(32) NULL,
  occurred_at TIMESTAMP NULL,
  created_by VARCHAR(45) NULL,
  created_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
  PRIMARY KEY (company_id, event_id),
  KEY idx_wse_submission (company_id, submission_id)
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4

CREATE TABLE waste_submission_result (
  company_id INT NOT NULL,
  result_id INT NOT NULL,
  event_id INT NOT NULL,
  severity VARCHAR(8) NOT NULL,
  code VARCHAR(64) NULL,
  description VARCHAR(1000) NULL,
  created_by VARCHAR(45) NULL,
  created_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
  PRIMARY KEY (company_id, result_id),
  KEY idx_wsr_event (company_id, event_id)
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4

-- Backfill: every albaran_ler that already has an esir_csv (a real prior eSIR
-- submission) gets one ACK row, so the idempotency guard is correct immediately.
-- Per-company submission_id = MAX(existing)+ROW_NUMBER(); the @AutoId generator
-- lazy-inits its counter from MAX+1 afterward, so no counter seeding is needed.
INSERT INTO waste_submission
  (company_id, submission_id, platform, document_type, document_number, status, csv,
   albaran_id, codigo_ler, residue_id, created_by, created_at, updated_by, updated_at)
SELECT al.company_id,
       (SELECT COALESCE(MAX(w.submission_id), 0)
          FROM waste_submission w WHERE w.company_id = al.company_id)
       + ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY al.company_id
                            ORDER BY al.albaran_id, al.codigo_ler, al.residue_id),
       'ESIR', 'DCS', al.numero_di, 'ACK', al.esir_csv,
       al.albaran_id, al.codigo_ler, al.residue_id,
       'migration', NOW(), 'migration', NOW()
FROM albaran_ler al
WHERE al.esir_csv IS NOT NULL
  AND al.numero_di IS NOT NULL
archive/2026-06-10_001_operator-grant-el:
-- Extend the operator-grant scope CHECK with the ELEVATION-ONLY marker scope ('elevation'):
-- elevation eligibility into the grant's company allow-list with ZERO platform permissions
-- (OperatorPermissions.ELEVATION_ONLY — deliberately not in OperatorPermissions.ALL, so the
-- permission union fail-closed-drops it). The "tenant-support operator" shape.
--
-- MySQL cannot ALTER a CHECK in place: drop + re-add. Idempotent both ways — the DROP only runs
-- if the constraint exists, and the re-add includes 'elevation' exactly once. Safe to re-run.

DROP PROCEDURE IF EXISTS _widen_operator_grant_scope_check

CREATE PROCEDURE _widen_operator_grant_scope_check()
BEGIN
    IF EXISTS (
        SELECT 1 FROM information_schema.TABLE_CONSTRAINTS
        WHERE CONSTRAINT_SCHEMA = DATABASE()
          AND TABLE_NAME = '_developer_operator_grant'
          AND CONSTRAINT_NAME = 'chk_operator_grant_scope'
    ) AND NOT EXISTS (
        SELECT 1 FROM information_schema.CHECK_CONSTRAINTS
        WHERE CONSTRAINT_SCHEMA = DATABASE()
          AND CONSTRAINT_NAME = 'chk_operator_grant_scope'
          AND CHECK_CLAUSE LIKE '%elevation%'
    ) THEN
        ALTER TABLE _developer_operator_grant DROP CONSTRAINT chk_operator_grant_scope;
    END IF;

    IF NOT EXISTS (
        SELECT 1 FROM information_schema.TABLE_CONSTRAINTS
        WHERE CONSTRAINT_SCHEMA = DATABASE()
          AND TABLE_NAME = '_developer_operator_grant'
          AND CONSTRAINT_NAME = 'chk_operator_grant_scope'
    ) THEN
        ALTER TABLE _developer_operator_grant
          ADD CONSTRAINT chk_operator_grant_scope
          CHECK (scope IS NULL OR scope IN ('sysadmin','printables','migration','scheduler','tracking','grants','elevation'));
    END IF;
END
archive/2026-06-18_001_dedupe-cuenta-lin:
-- Data cleanup: collapse duplicate grouping lines in `cuenta_linea`.
--
-- BACKGROUND
-- A "grouping line" is a `cuenta_linea` row with `grouping_line != 0`; other lines
-- attach to it via `group_with = <grouping line's line_id>`. The invariant is ONE
-- grouping line per (company_id, account_id, fecha, grouping_line). It was only ever
-- enforced procedurally: PaymentProcessingService.findGroupingLine looked the grouping
-- line up INDIRECTLY (via a child whose group_with pointed at it), so a grouping line
-- with no committed child yet was invisible. When a payable carried two medios for the
-- same account+date, the two createNewLine() iterations in one transaction each failed
-- the lookup and created a SECOND grouping line — producing the production
-- NonUniqueResultException on POST /private/albaran/{id}/pagar.
--
-- The race is fixed in code (direct lookup + flush + in-transaction guard). This script
-- repairs EXISTING duplicates so the data once again satisfies the invariant.
--
-- STRATEGY (per duplicate group, set-based, idempotent)
--   1. Survivor = the grouping line with the lowest line_id in the group (matches the
--      new code's `order by cl.pk.lineId asc` so future lookups land on the same row).
--   2. Re-point every child line (group_with) from a duplicate to the survivor.
--   3. Delete the now-orphaned duplicate grouping lines.
--   4. Recompute the survivor's `cantidad` = SUM(child.cantidad) (mirrors
--      EntityManager.getGroupedLinesSum). Recompute ALL survivors that owned a duplicate.
--
-- Multi-tenant safe: grouping is keyed by company_id throughout. Re-runnable: once the
-- duplicates are gone, the duplicate CTE is empty and every statement is a no-op.

-- --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- Map each duplicate grouping line to the survivor it should be merged into.
-- A "duplicate" is any grouping line in a (company_id, account_id, fecha, grouping_line)
-- group of size > 1 that is NOT the lowest line_id in that group.
-- --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DROP TEMPORARY TABLE IF EXISTS tmp_grouping_dedupe

CREATE TEMPORARY TABLE tmp_grouping_dedupe AS
SELECT
    g.company_id,
    g.line_id              AS dup_line_id,      -- grouping line to remove
    s.survivor_line_id     AS survivor_line_id  -- grouping line to keep
FROM cuenta_linea g
JOIN (
    -- survivor = MIN(line_id) per duplicate key
    SELECT company_id, account_id, fecha, grouping_line,
           MIN(line_id) AS survivor_line_id
    FROM cuenta_linea
    WHERE grouping_line != 0
    GROUP BY company_id, account_id, fecha, grouping_line
    HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
) s
  ON  g.company_id    = s.company_id
  AND g.account_id    = s.account_id
  AND g.fecha         = s.fecha
  AND g.grouping_line = s.grouping_line
WHERE g.grouping_line != 0
  AND g.line_id <> s.survivor_line_id

-- keep the survivor, list only the duplicates

-- --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- Distinct survivors that absorbed at least one duplicate (used for the cantidad refresh).
-- --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DROP TEMPORARY TABLE IF EXISTS tmp_grouping_survivors

CREATE TEMPORARY TABLE tmp_grouping_survivors AS
SELECT DISTINCT company_id, survivor_line_id AS line_id
FROM tmp_grouping_dedupe

-- --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- 1) Re-point children of the duplicate grouping lines onto the survivor.
-- --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
UPDATE cuenta_linea child
JOIN tmp_grouping_dedupe d
  ON  child.company_id = d.company_id
  AND child.group_with = d.dup_line_id
SET child.group_with = d.survivor_line_id

-- --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- 2) Delete the orphaned duplicate grouping lines (children already moved).
-- --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DELETE c
FROM cuenta_linea c
JOIN tmp_grouping_dedupe d
  ON  c.company_id = d.company_id
  AND c.line_id    = d.dup_line_id

-- --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- 3) Recompute the survivors' cantidad from their (now merged) children.
--    Mirrors getGroupedLinesSum: SUM over child lines where group_with = survivor.
--    The sums are materialized into a temp table first because MySQL forbids an UPDATE
--    whose subquery selects from the table being updated (ERROR 1093).
-- --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DROP TEMPORARY TABLE IF EXISTS tmp_grouping_sums

CREATE TEMPORARY TABLE tmp_grouping_sums AS
SELECT t.company_id,
       t.line_id,
       COALESCE(SUM(ch.cantidad), 0) AS total
FROM tmp_grouping_survivors t
LEFT JOIN cuenta_linea ch
  ON ch.company_id = t.company_id
  AND ch.group_with = t.line_id
GROUP BY t.company_id, t.line_id

UPDATE cuenta_linea s
JOIN tmp_grouping_sums g
  ON s.company_id = g.company_id AND s.line_id = g.line_id
SET s.cantidad = g.total
DROP TEMPORARY TABLE IF EXISTS tmp_grouping_dedupe
DROP TEMPORARY TABLE IF EXISTS tmp_grouping_survivors
DROP TEMPORARY TABLE IF EXISTS tmp_grouping_sums
archive/2026-06-18_002_factura-face.sql:
-- FACe submission tracking (mirrors factura_sii). One row per send ATTEMPT:
-- written PENDING before the SOAP call, updated to SENT/ERROR after.
-- Composite @AutoId PK → NO AUTO_INCREMENT (the app allocates face_id).
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS factura_face (
    company_id        INT          NOT NULL,
    invoice_id        INT          NOT NULL,
    face_id           INT          NOT NULL,
    fecha             DATE         NULL,
    estado_envio      VARCHAR(16)  NULL,
    registry_code     VARCHAR(64)  NULL,
    estado_registro   VARCHAR(64)  NULL,
    codigo_error      VARCHAR(32)  NULL,
    descripcion_error VARCHAR(512) NULL,
    respuesta         MEDIUMTEXT   NULL,
    timestamp_envio   DATETIME(3)  NULL,
    pruebas           TINYINT(1)   NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    created_by        VARCHAR(45)  CHARACTER SET utf8mb4 COLLATE utf8mb4_unicode_ci DEFAULT NULL,
    created_at        DATETIME(3)  NULL,
    updated_by        VARCHAR(45)  CHARACTER SET utf8mb4 COLLATE utf8mb4_unicode_ci DEFAULT NULL,
    updated_at        DATETIME(3)  NULL,
    PRIMARY KEY (company_id, invoice_id, face_id),
    KEY idx_factura_face_registry (company_id, registry_code),
    CONSTRAINT fk_factura_face_factura
        FOREIGN KEY (company_id, invoice_id) REFERENCES factura (company_id, invoice_id)
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci
archive/2026-06-21_001_scheduler-executi:

archive/2026-06-21_002_scheduler-notific:
-- Make scheduler notification creation a single-winner under concurrent runs (Q5 F4).
--
-- Pre-flight: run the following query before applying and confirm it returns 0 rows.
-- If duplicate rows exist, dedup first (keep lowest modulo_notificacion_id per group):
--   SELECT company_id, module, notification, fecha, COUNT(*)
--   FROM modulo_notificacion
--   GROUP BY company_id, module, notification, fecha
--   HAVING COUNT(*) > 1;
--
-- Pre-flight confirmed: 0 duplicate rows on 2026-06-21 before this constraint was added.
CALL zn_add_unique_if_missing('modulo_notificacion', 'uq_modnotif_company_module_notif_date', 'company_id, module, notification, fecha')
archive/2026-06-24_001_waste-submission-:
-- Phase 2 recovery: stale marker + sweep index.
-- reserved_at is set when a submission enters SUBMITTING (the recovery stale marker;
-- submitted_at is null while SUBMITTING so it cannot drive the sweep). No backfill:
-- a null reserved_at on a pre-existing SUBMITTING row is treated as eligible (old).
CALL zn_add_column_if_missing('waste_submission', 'reserved_at', 'TIMESTAMP NULL AFTER submitted_at')
archive/2026-06-24_002_waste-submission-:
-- Fix the collation mismatch across the whole waste_submission* table family.
-- The 2026-06-08_001 migration created these tables with DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 but NO
-- explicit COLLATE, so every string column inherited the MySQL-8 server default
-- (utf8mb4_0900_ai_ci) instead of the project-wide utf8mb4_unicode_ci. Any join from a
-- waste_submission* string column to another table (e.g. the AlbaranLer.wasteSubmissionStatus
-- @Computed subquery joining codigo_ler) hits "Illegal mix of collations".
--
-- codigo_ler is the only column that breaks TODAY (the only one joined cross-table), but
-- document_number / created_by / status / etc. are latent landmines for the next join. Fix the
-- root cause: CONVERT each table to utf8mb4_unicode_ci so ALL columns match, not just one.
-- (A table rebuild — briefly locks — but the correct one-shot fix.)
--
-- CONVERT TO CHARACTER SET is idempotent: running it on an already-correct table is a no-op.
ALTER TABLE waste_submission        CONVERT TO CHARACTER SET utf8mb4 COLLATE utf8mb4_unicode_ci
ALTER TABLE waste_submission_event  CONVERT TO CHARACTER SET utf8mb4 COLLATE utf8mb4_unicode_ci
ALTER TABLE waste_submission_result CONVERT TO CHARACTER SET utf8mb4 COLLATE utf8mb4_unicode_ci
archive/2026-06-25_001_waste-routing.sql:
-- Phase 4: per-center / per-tenant waste-platform routing target.
-- Nullable; null = inherit (center → empresa → ESIR). Holds a WastePlatform name.
-- Explicit COLLATE (Phase-3 lesson: omitting it inherits the MySQL-8 server default).
CALL zn_add_column_if_missing('entidad_centro', 'waste_submission_target', 'VARCHAR(16) CHARACTER SET utf8mb4 COLLATE utf8mb4_unicode_ci NULL')
CALL zn_add_column_if_missing('empresa', 'waste_submission_target', 'VARCHAR(16) CHARACTER SET utf8mb4 COLLATE utf8mb4_unicode_ci NULL')
archive/2026-06-25_002_credencial-tipo-g:
-- Phase 4: expand the credencial tipo CHECK constraints to allow GAIA.
-- The original (2026-05-27_001) restricts to AEAT/ESIR/MAIL/WEBHOOK; a GAIA
-- credential INSERT fails at the DB without this.
-- zn_replace_check: drops the named constraint if it exists, then re-creates it.
CALL zn_replace_check('empresa_credencial', 'chk_credencial_tipo', "tipo IN ('AEAT', 'ESIR', 'MAIL', 'WEBHOOK', 'GAIA')")
archive/2026-06-26_001_scheduler-notific:
-- Single-winner scheduler notification creation under concurrent runs (Q5 F4).
--
-- Carried forward from the pre-reset 2026-06-21_002 — the ONE schema change not
-- yet applied to production at the 2026-06-26 migration-system reset. Every other
-- historical migration is already in the frozen baseline (a real prod snapshot);
-- this one is not, because production accumulated duplicate rows that block the
-- constraint, so it was never applied.
--
-- SchedulerNotificationHelper depends on this UNIQUE constraint as (a) its
-- single-winner tiebreaker under concurrent scheduler runs (catches MySQL 1062 →
-- "already created concurrently") and (b) the only serialization for its
-- MAX(mod_nt_id)+1 id generation (EclipseLink cannot lock a scalar aggregate).
--
-- SELF-CONTAINED: the dedup is part of the migration. A modulo_notificacion row
-- is a per-(company, module, notification, day) *badge* ("there is HR-holiday
-- activity today"), not a per-request record — the actual leave requests live in
-- empleado_ausencia_peticion. So duplicate (company, module, notification, fecha)
-- rows are redundant by definition (the race this constraint prevents); collapsing
-- them to one loses no real data. Keep the lowest mod_nt_id per group.

-- 1. Remove duplicate rows that would violate the new UNIQUE constraint.
DELETE n1 FROM modulo_notificacion n1
  INNER JOIN modulo_notificacion n2
    ON  n1.company_id  = n2.company_id
    AND n1.module       = n2.module
    AND n1.notification = n2.notification
    AND n1.fecha        = n2.fecha
    AND n1.mod_nt_id    > n2.mod_nt_id

-- 2. Add the constraint (idempotent — skipped if already present).
CALL zn_add_unique_if_missing('modulo_notificacion',
  'uq_modnotif_company_module_notif_date',
  'company_id, module, notification, fecha')

-- 3. Postcondition: prove the constraint exists.
CALL zn_assert_index('modulo_notificacion', 'uq_modnotif_company_module_notif_date')
archive/2026-06-26_002_waste-routing.sql:
-- Carried forward from archive/2026-06-25_001_waste-routing.sql at the 2026-06-26 reset:
